Simple calculator template: total errors divided by total orders served, multiplied by 100, with category breakdowns and comparison against target and previous weeks.
Structured template for investigating recurring error types: description of the error pattern, possible causes, evidence review, root cause determination, fix implemented, and follow-up plan.
Monthly report showing total orders, total errors, error rate, category breakdown, trend over time, root causes addressed, fixes implemented, and their impact.
Quarterly analysis examining error patterns by time of day, day of week, staff member, menu item, and order complexity, with recommendations for systemic improvements.
Simple daily tally sheet for counting recognised returning customers alongside total transaction numbers, giving a daily return rate estimate.
Monthly report template showing: total unique customers, new versus returning ratio, visit frequency distribution, lapsed customer count, estimated churn, and revenue concentration.
List of identified regular customers who have not visited in 4+ weeks, with last visit date, usual order, and any known reasons for absence.
Calculator for key retention metrics: return rate, average visit frequency, estimated customer lifetime value, and churn rate based on visit data.
Template for quarterly retention deep-dive: trend analysis, external factor assessment, impact of business changes on retention, and strategic recommendations.
Calculator for determining programme cost per transaction, break-even visit frequency, and ROI based on stamp count, reward value, and estimated participation rate.
Standard Operating Procedure covering: how to offer and issue cards, stamping/scanning procedure, redemption rules, handling lost cards, programme rules, and staff FAQs.
Dashboard tracking active members, new sign-ups, stamp accumulation rate, redemptions, lapsed cards, and participation rate as a percentage of total customers.
Annual evaluation template assessing programme effectiveness: participation trends, impact on visit frequency, cost versus revenue, customer feedback, and recommendations for enhancement.
Comparison matrix for evaluating digital loyalty platforms: features, monthly cost, POS integration, customer experience, data capabilities, and suitability for independent cafes.
Behind-the-counter reference listing each regular customer: name, usual order, personal details known, preferences, and notes. Updated regularly and shared across the team.
Training guide covering natural name-learning techniques, appropriate use of personal details, the 'usual or something different?' approach, and avoiding performative recognition.
Brief protocol for the pre-shift huddle: team members share any notable customer information — holidays, preferences changes, milestones — to enable personalised service.
Assessment testing each team member's ability to name regular customers and their usual orders. Tracks team recognition capability and identifies training needs.
Planning template for annual regulars appreciation events: format options, budget, invitation list, menu, timing, and event logistics.
Calculator for estimating CLV by customer segment: average spend per visit, visits per week, weeks per year, and estimated relationship duration in years.
Simple one-page document explaining CLV to the team: what it means, how much a regular customer is worth, and how this changes the way we think about service and problem resolution.
Template for planning initiatives to improve each CLV lever: current baseline, target improvement, specific actions, expected revenue impact, and investment required.
Calculator for quantifying the revenue impact of customer churn: number of regulars lost per month, their average CLV, annual revenue lost, and break-even investment in retention.
Quarterly report template showing CLV by segment, trends over time, lever performance (spend, frequency, duration), churn impact, and ROI of retention investments.
Laminated card posted at service points showing the LAST framework: Listen, Apologise, Solve, Thank — with brief guidance for each step.
Daily log for recording every customer complaint: date, time, nature of complaint, customer's words, resolution offered, staff member who resolved it, and customer's response.
Standard Operating Procedure covering the LAST framework, empowerment thresholds, escalation procedures, difficult customer handling, follow-up practices, and documentation.
Guide defining specific resolution options for each complaint type: replacements, refunds, complimentary items, apology approaches, and follow-up actions.
Quarterly analysis of complaint patterns: volume, categories, time distribution, root causes, resolution effectiveness, recovery rate, and comparison with online review themes.
Small card or table talker with a QR code linking to the Google review page and a brief, friendly prompt to leave a review. Placed on tables and at the till.
Unified log capturing all customer feedback from all channels: date, source, type (compliment/complaint/suggestion), detail, action taken, and follow-up status.
Standard Operating Procedure covering how to respond to online reviews, how to record in-person feedback, how to process feedback cards, and how to share insights with the team.
Monthly report summarising all feedback by channel and type, identifying top themes, tracking star rating trends, and listing actionable improvements with implementation status.
Simple quarterly survey template centred on the Net Promoter Score question (0-10 recommendation likelihood) plus 2-3 follow-up questions, designed for quick in-cafe or digital completion.
A5 card kept behind the counter for logging any customer complaint: date, time, nature of issue, tier classification, action taken, staff member, and customer reaction. Reviewed daily by manager.
Comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure covering the full recovery cycle: detection, acknowledgement, resolution, follow-up, root cause logging, and systemic improvement actions.
Digital tracker categorising complaints by type, severity, time, shift, and resolution. Generates weekly summaries showing trends and recurring issues for management review.
Template frameworks for responding to negative Google, TripAdvisor, and social media reviews. Includes tone guidelines, personalisation requirements, and response time targets.
Monthly summary analysing complaint volumes, categories, resolution times, recovery budget spend, complaint-to-advocacy conversion rate, and online review sentiment trends.
Structured checklist for walking the complete customer journey from street approach to departure, noting observations at each of the ten key stages. Used during initial mapping and quarterly reviews.
Document defining measurable standards for each critical journey touchpoint including greeting time, order accuracy, drink delivery speed, farewell quality, and cleanliness standards.
Periodic audit tool where the manager scores each journey stage (1-5) during a shift, noting specific observations and improvement opportunities. Results feed into monthly reviews.
Short three-question survey card used with departing customers to rate their experience at one specific touchpoint. Rotated weekly to build comprehensive journey feedback.
Monthly summary combining touchpoint audit scores, intercept survey data, and online review sentiment to show journey performance trends and identify priority improvement areas.

Structured checklist covering physical access (entrance, pathways, counter, seating, toilets), communication (menu formats, staff interaction), sensory environment, and digital presence. Used for initial audit and annual reviews.
Prioritised list of accessibility barriers identified during the audit, categorised by fix timeframe (immediate, this month, longer-term) with assigned responsibility and completion status.
Training materials covering the social model of disability, Equality Act 2010 duties, communication techniques for different needs, scenario-based role-plays, and appropriate language guidance.
Simple feedback form (physical and digital) specifically for customers to report accessibility barriers or suggest improvements, designed to be low-pressure and available in accessible formats.
Comprehensive annual report documenting current accessibility provisions, improvements made, remaining barriers, customer feedback, staff training completion, and next year's improvement plan.
Facilitation guide for the team workshop including the three sentence-completion exercises, customer interview questions, and a framework for identifying authentic strengths from the responses.
Structured template for auditing competitor cafes covering coffee quality, food offering, atmosphere, service style, pricing, unique features, and identified gaps or opportunities.

Shared board or document tracking experience innovation ideas from proposal through to trial, evaluation, and decision (adopt, adapt, or abandon). Includes fields for idea source, cost, trial period, and measured outcomes.
Rolling three-month calendar planning seasonal drinks, themed promotions, events, collaborations, and content moments. Includes preparation deadlines and responsibility assignments.

Quarterly report tracking experience-related metrics: social media mentions and tags, customer comments about specific experience elements, innovation trial outcomes, event attendance, and dwell time trends.
Daily log recording grind setting, dose, yield, shot time, tasting notes, and adjustments made. Tracks who performed dial-in and confirms quality was verified before opening.
Laminated A6 card mounted on or near the grinder displaying the current espresso recipe: dose, yield, target time, ratio, and acceptable tolerance ranges. Updated when the coffee changes.

Comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure covering the full dial-in process: machine warm-up, purging, grinding, distribution, tamping, extraction, weighing, timing, tasting, adjusting, and communicating results to the team.
Reference card defining the key espresso tasting terms — sour, bitter, sweet, body, acidity, finish — with examples and guidance on what each indicates about extraction quality.
Monthly chart plotting grind settings over time against bean batch changes, environmental conditions, and maintenance events. Used to predict adjustments and evaluate bean consistency.
A4 laminated reference showing target parameters (dose, yield, time), visual examples of good/bad shots, crema standards, and the sink shot policy. Posted at the espresso station.
Log for recording random shot quality checks during service: barista name, time, measured parameters, tasting notes, pass/fail, and feedback given. Used for trend analysis and training decisions.
Comprehensive procedure covering portafilter preparation, dosing, distribution, tamping, extraction monitoring, visual assessment, tasting evaluation, and troubleshooting common quality issues.
Practical assessment checklist for evaluating a barista's ability to consistently pull shots within target parameters, accurately assess quality, and make appropriate grind adjustments.
Weekly summary of quality check results, sink shot rates by barista and time period, competency assessment outcomes, and trends in extraction consistency.
End-of-day checklist for each grinder: hopper emptied, chamber brushed, dosing area cleaned, exterior wiped, log entry made. Part of the closing routine, signed off by the closing barista.
Ongoing log recording all maintenance activities: daily cleans, weekly deep cleans, monthly inspections, burr changes, and professional services. Tracks who performed each task and any issues found.

Comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure detailing daily cleaning steps, weekly deep clean procedure, monthly burr inspection, throughput tracking, burr replacement process, and annual professional service requirements.
Spreadsheet or template that calculates burr replacement dates based on daily coffee throughput, burr type, and manufacturer recommendations. Sends alerts when replacement is approaching.
Complete lifetime service record for each grinder: purchase date, all maintenance performed, parts replaced, professional service reports, performance notes, and photographic burr inspection records.
Step-by-step guide for conducting team cupping sessions: equipment needed, brewing ratios, tasting protocol, scoring framework, and discussion prompts. Designed for 20-minute fortnightly sessions.
Practical assessment checklists for three skill levels (Competent, Proficient, Advanced) covering espresso preparation, sensory evaluation, troubleshooting, and training capability.
Monthly template integrating dial-in data, quality check results, grinder maintenance records, customer feedback, and cupping notes into a single quality overview with trend analysis.
Log tracking all communications with the coffee roaster: feedback shared, questions raised, recipe adjustments discussed, roastery visits, and samples received.

Annual review template covering equipment condition and upgrade needs, roaster evaluation, barista development progress, quality metric trends, customer satisfaction, and programme goals for the coming year.
Laminated A5 card posted at the espresso station showing the three steaming phases, target temperatures for each drink type, and the five-point quality check. Quick visual reference during service.

Comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure covering the full steaming process: jug selection, filling, stretching, texturing, temperature control, common faults and corrections, and waste minimisation.
Visual and descriptive guide showing common milk steaming faults (too foamy, too thin, scalded, bubbly, separated) with causes and corrections. Used for training and self-diagnosis.
Practical assessment checklist evaluating a barista's steaming technique: positioning, aeration duration, texture quality, temperature accuracy, waste management, and steam wand hygiene.
Weekly tracker comparing milk purchased against drinks served to calculate usage ratio. Identifies waste trends and correlates with barista shifts to pinpoint training opportunities.
Laminated card at the espresso station showing steaming temperature, stretch duration, pour technique, and known issues for each alternative milk type stocked. Updated when brands change.
Detailed Standard Operating Procedure covering steaming technique, temperature targets, pour sequences, allergen considerations, and brand-specific notes for oat, soy, almond, and coconut milks.
Structured tasting form for evaluating new alternative milk products on: steaming performance, foam quality, taste with espresso, latte art capability, shelf stability, cost, and allergen profile.
Staff guide covering allergen content of each alternative milk stocked (soy allergen, gluten in oat, tree nuts in almond/coconut), what to tell customers, and when to advise caution.

tools.tool_name: Alternative Milk Sales and Waste Tracker
tools.description: Monthly tracker comparing alternative milk purchases, usage per drink, waste volumes, and sales trends. Used to optimise ordering quantities and brand selection.
A3 poster displayed in the staff area showing the minimum acceptable latte art standard with reference photos of heart, rosetta, and tulip patterns at the cafe's quality level.
Standard Operating Procedure covering pouring technique for three fundamental patterns, cup angle, flow control, common mistakes and corrections, and guidance for different cup sizes.
Practical assessment checklist for evaluating latte art at three skill levels: consistency, symmetry, definition, contrast, and pattern complexity. Used for progression tracking.

tools.tool_name: Monthly Latte Art Challenge Score Sheet
tools.description: Scoring template for monthly in-house latte art challenges: criteria for definition, symmetry, contrast, creativity, and overall visual impact. Includes space for photographs.
Ongoing log tracking latte art quality during random service checks, competition results, social media engagement on latte art posts, and individual barista progression through skill levels.
Visual matrix showing each barista's current skill level across dairy steaming, alternative milk handling, and latte art. Updated monthly and used for development planning.
Personal development plan for each barista covering current skill levels, 30-day goals, practice focus areas, and mentor assignment. Reviewed and updated monthly.
Complete assessment pack with practical test criteria for four mastery levels. Covers dairy steaming accuracy, alternative milk competence, latte art quality, waste management, and training ability.
Personal log where baristas record practice sessions: date, duration, techniques practised, self-assessment of results, and areas for improvement. Reviewed during monthly development conversations.
Quarterly report tracking programme outcomes: skill progression rates, waste reduction, customer satisfaction trends, social media engagement from milk craft content, and staff retention correlation.

Record of initial mains water testing: hardness, TDS, pH, and chlorine levels. Establishes the baseline against which filtered water and future tests are compared. Updated annually or when mains supply changes.
Tracker showing filter installation date, expected replacement date based on calculated water usage, actual replacement dates, and cartridge model/supplier information.

Standard Operating Procedure covering weekly TDS testing, monthly comprehensive testing, filter replacement procedure, escalation when readings are out of range, and communication with the machine technician.
Simple weekly log recording TDS readings from the machine's hot water output, compared against the baseline and SCA recommended range. Triggers investigation when readings deviate beyond 20%.
Annual report summarising water quality data trends, filter performance, equipment health correlation, costs, and recommendations for filtration system improvements or adjustments.
Step-by-step closing checklist for backflushing each group head, cleaning shower screens and baskets, steam wand care, drip tray cleaning, and external wipe-down. Signed off by the closing barista.
Ongoing log recording all machine maintenance: daily backflushes, weekly inspections, gasket replacements, professional services, parts replaced, and any issues observed or reported.

Comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure covering daily backflushing, weekly deep cleaning, monthly gasket inspection, and annual professional service requirements with step-by-step procedures for each.

Visual decision tree for diagnosing common machine issues: low pressure, temperature inconsistency, leaking group, poor steam, and unusual sounds. Guides the user from symptom to likely cause and appropriate action.

Quarterly assessment measuring boiler pressure stability, group head temperature consistency, pump pressure accuracy, flow rate, steam pressure, and visual condition. Tracks trends over time to predict maintenance needs.
Template for recording comprehensive laboratory water analysis results: mineral composition, hardness, alkalinity, TDS, pH, and comparison against SCA and manufacturer specifications.
Strategic plan documenting the cafe's water profile, optimisation objectives, filtration adjustments needed, testing protocols, and timeline for achieving target water quality.
Monthly testing log recording TDS, hardness, and pH from three sample points (mains inlet, filter outlet, group head) with trend analysis and seasonal variation notes.
Advanced tracker correlating water mineral data with espresso extraction percentages, sensory scores, and dial-in difficulty. Used to understand and optimise the water-coffee relationship.
Annual comprehensive review of the water management system: mains water trends, filter performance, machine condition, coffee quality correlation, costs, and strategic recommendations.
A5 laminated card for each food item showing ingredients, exact quantities, step-by-step preparation, cooking parameters, plating instructions, reference photo, and allergen information.
Daily checklist of all items to be prepared before service, with quantities adjusted for anticipated demand. Signed off by the kitchen opener when prep is complete.
Complete manual documenting preparation procedures for every food item on the menu, including recipes, photos, allergen data, storage requirements, and shelf life information.
Quick-reference checklist for the visual quality check before food leaves the kitchen: correct portion, proper presentation, correct temperature, and matches the prep card reference photo.
Monthly report comparing actual food costs against theoretical costs based on prep card recipes and sales volume. Highlights variance by item and identifies portioning, waste, or procurement issues.
12-month visual calendar showing planned seasonal items for each period, key ingredient availability windows, launch dates, and marketing milestones. Updated annually and reviewed quarterly.

Structured template guiding seasonal recipe development: flavour profile, ingredient list with costs, preparation method, allergen assessment, presentation design, and tasting panel evaluation criteria.
Evaluation form for the tasting panel to assess new seasonal items on: taste, visual appeal, value perception, operational feasibility, allergen clarity, and overall recommendation to launch.
Tracker recording daily sales, customer feedback, social media engagement, waste volumes, and gross margin for each seasonal item throughout its availability period.

Annual review comparing all seasonal items' performance: sales ranking, profitability, customer response, waste efficiency, and recommendations for next year's calendar including 'greatest hits' returns.
Complete register of all food and drink suppliers: contact details, products supplied, order and delivery schedule, payment terms, allergen documentation status, and backup alternatives.

Quarterly assessment form rating each supplier on quality, reliability, documentation, pricing, and relationship. Calculates an overall score with trigger thresholds for review or replacement actions.
Delivery check sheet for recording: order versus delivery comparison, visual quality assessment, temperature check of chilled/frozen goods, use-by date verification, and any discrepancies noted.
Log recording any delivery issues: short deliveries, quality problems, temperature failures, or incorrect items. Tracks supplier response and resolution for use in quarterly assessments.
Structured template for annual supplier review meetings: performance summary, scorecard trends, pricing discussion, product development opportunities, and action items for the coming year.

Structured template for auditing every food item on the menu: taste, presentation, temperature, portion size, value for money, and overall quality rating (1-5). Used for initial audit and quarterly reviews.

Tracker managing the lifecycle of new food items from idea through recipe development, costing, tasting panel approval, staff training, and launch. Includes fields for status, cost data, and launch dates.
Template for structured competitor food evaluations: items tasted, quality assessment, presentation, pricing, portions, and lessons or ideas for your own menu development.
Standing agenda for the monthly food quality meeting: menu item tasting, customer feedback review, cost data analysis, seasonal planning, improvement actions, and team skill development planning.

Annual report assessing the food programme's performance: quality scores, food revenue contribution, cost efficiency, customer satisfaction, awards and recognition, team development, and goals for the coming year.

A5 template for creating drink recipe cards: drink name, sizes, espresso specification, milk/water quantities, additions with measurements, preparation sequence, serving vessel, garnish, allergens, and reference photo.
Complete list of every drink available in every size and variation (milk type, iced, decaf). Used as the index for recipe cards and for menu board accuracy verification.
Standard Operating Procedure for recipe card management: creation process, approval workflow, update triggers, team briefing requirements, and retirement protocol for discontinued drinks.
Observation checklist for verifying barista adherence to recipe cards during service: correct dose, correct milk quantity, correct additions, correct preparation sequence, and correct presentation.
Spreadsheet calculating the cost per drink based on recipe card quantities and current ingredient prices. Used for pricing decisions, menu engineering, and margin analysis.
Complete document listing every food and drink item with exact portion sizes for every ingredient in grams or millilitres. The single source of truth for what goes into every serve.
Laminated A4 photos showing correct portions of high-cost ingredients on plates and in cups, with weight annotations. Posted in kitchen and bar for quick reference during service.
Log for recording random portioning checks during service: item checked, specified portion, actual measured portion, variance, and any coaching action taken.
Template for weekly yield testing of high-cost ingredients: amount purchased, theoretical usage (serves x portion), actual remaining stock, calculated variance, and investigation notes.

Monthly report comparing theoretical food costs (based on portion standards and sales data) against actual food costs (based on purchases). Highlights variance by ingredient category and identifies trends.
Spreadsheet plotting every menu item by popularity (weekly sales) and contribution margin, automatically categorising items as Stars, Plowhorses, Puzzles, or Dogs. Updated quarterly.
Calculator for determining each menu item's contribution margin: selling price minus ingredient cost per serve. Uses cost data from recipe cards (B054) and portion standards (B055).
Guide covering menu design principles: sweet spot positioning, pricing psychology, font and colour choices, category organisation, and menu size optimisation for cafes.
Quick-reference card for front-of-house staff listing current Puzzle items to recommend, suggested phrasing for upselling, and bundling options. Updated quarterly after matrix analysis.
Quarterly report showing matrix categorisation changes, contribution margin trends, average transaction value, pricing analysis, and recommendations for menu adjustments.
A4 log sheet placed near the waste bin: columns for time, item, quantity, reason category, and staff initials. Completed in real-time as waste occurs. Reviewed daily by manager with cost annotation.
Spreadsheet or app tracking all waste entries with automatic calculations for daily, weekly, and monthly values, category breakdowns, most-wasted items, and trend charts.
10-minute meeting template: review total waste value, discuss top three most-wasted items, brainstorm solutions, assign actions, and compare against previous week's performance.
Physical bin audit checklist for end-of-day waste inspection: categorise, weigh, and record all waste found. Compare against the daily log to assess recording accuracy.
Monthly report showing waste value, waste percentage of purchases, category breakdown, most-wasted items, trend over time, environmental impact estimate, and comparison against WRAP targets.
Structured document listing the top waste items, root cause analysis for each, specific reduction interventions, responsible persons, target dates, and expected savings.
Production planning sheet showing the target preparation quantity for each item by day of week, based on historical sales data. Adjusted monthly as demand patterns shift seasonally.
Standard Operating Procedure for first-in-first-out stock rotation: date marking on receipt, storage positioning, prep usage sequence, and weekly date audit procedure.
Weekly inspection checklist for all perishable stock: verify date labels present, check use-by dates, identify items within 48 hours of expiry for priority use, and flag items for disposal.
Monthly report tracking waste percentage trend, progress against targets, financial savings achieved, redistribution volumes (Too Good To Go, donations), and environmental impact metrics.

Structured template for auditing every waste stream in the cafe: food, packaging, general, recyclable, and specialty waste. Records volume estimates, current disposal methods, and immediate improvement opportunities.
Comprehensive plan covering waste, packaging, energy, water, and supply chain sustainability. Includes targets, interventions, timelines, responsibilities, and cost implications.
Audit of every single-use item in the cafe with assessment of sustainable alternatives: compostable, recyclable, or reusable options with cost comparison and supplier information.
Tracker for the green team's sustainability initiatives: ideas proposed, actions taken, outcomes measured, and next steps. Updated monthly and reviewed in team meetings.
Annual report publishing the cafe's sustainability metrics: waste diverted from landfill, energy consumption trends, cups saved, carbon footprint estimate, and progress against zero-waste targets.
Grid showing every menu item against the 14 major allergens, marking which are present. Includes dietary suitability columns (vegan, vegetarian, GF, DF). Updated whenever recipes or suppliers change.
Standard Operating Procedure covering allergen management, dietary labelling, cross-contamination prevention, supplier communication, staff training, and customer interaction protocols.

Practical test assessing each staff member's ability to answer common dietary questions: identifying allergens in specific menu items, recommending options for dietary restrictions, and explaining cross-contamination risks.
Assessment documenting cross-contamination risks in the kitchen and bar: shared equipment, shared preparation surfaces, shared storage, and the controls or communications in place for each risk.
Annual review of dietary accommodation: menu coverage across dietary categories, customer feedback trends, staff training compliance, allergen incident log, and recommendations for menu development.
Systematic audit of every current menu item categorised as suitable, adaptable, or not feasible for vegan and GF diets, with allergen status verification.
Step-by-step protocol covering storage, preparation, cooking, and serving procedures to prevent allergen cross-contamination in a cafe kitchen environment.
Structured recipe development template for new vegan/GF items including ingredient costing, allergen declaration, preparation method, shelf life, and quality benchmarks.
Monthly tracking dashboard for dietary item sales volume, percentage of total revenue, customer feedback scores, and waste rates to inform menu development decisions.
Audit of all customer touchpoints (menus, signage, website, social media, platforms) to identify gaps in dietary option visibility and communication.
Monthly content planning template ensuring dietary items are featured regularly across social media, email, and in-venue communications.
Short feedback form for dietary customers covering menu satisfaction, communication clarity, confidence in allergen safety, and suggestions for improvement.
Monthly dashboard tracking dietary item sales trends, new customer attribution, online review sentiment for dietary mentions, and platform listing performance.
Structured exit interview form with core questions covering reasons for leaving, management experience, development opportunities, and employer recommendation likelihood.
Comprehensive tracking tool recording every departure with dates, tenure, categorised reasons, interview themes, and avoidability assessment for trend analysis.
Calculator that quantifies the true cost of each departure including recruitment, training, productivity loss, and team impact to build the business case for retention investment.
Structured quarterly report template presenting turnover data, trend analysis, root cause themes, cost impact, and recommended retention actions.

Confidential five-question survey covering job satisfaction, management relationship, workload fairness, development opportunities, and likelihood of staying 12+ months. Includes a shorter three-question quarterly version for ongoing tracking.
Structured monthly conversation guide covering wins, challenges, development interests, wellbeing check, and concerns. Designed to facilitate genuine dialogue rather than task-based status updates.

Single-page document capturing every element of the employment proposition — pay, tips, free drinks, meals, training, flexibility, social events, discounts, and intangible benefits. Used to articulate value to current and prospective staff.
Personalised retention plan for key team members documenting their motivations, concerns, career aspirations, and specific actions the cafe will take to address their needs over the next 3-6 months.

Structured exit interview form capturing the real reason for departure, preventability assessment, and recommendations. Includes a rolling tracker of voluntary turnover rate, cost per departure, and pattern analysis over time.
Spreadsheet capturing current venue pay rates alongside three to five local competitors per role, with columns for hourly rate, tips estimate, benefits value, and total compensation comparison.
Per-role calculator that converts all compensation elements — base pay, tips, pension, holiday, meals, drinks, training — into a single total hourly value for transparent communication.
Written tips policy compliant with the Employment (Allocation of Tips) Act 2023, covering collection method, pooling rules, allocation formula, distribution schedule, and record-keeping requirements.
Defines pay bands for each role tier with minimum, midpoint, and maximum rates, progression criteria, and review triggers. Links to career progression framework.

Individual annual statement for each team member showing the full monetary value of their compensation package including base pay, tips, pension contributions, holiday value, training investment, and perks.

Anonymous five-to-seven-question survey covering pay satisfaction, schedule fairness, management quality, team atmosphere, and future outlook. Designed for quarterly deployment via Google Forms or paper.
Monthly tracker capturing turnover rate, average tenure, reasons for leaving categorised by theme, cost per leaver, time to fill vacancies, and trend analysis over rolling 12 months.
Structured question set for six-month and twelve-month stay interviews focusing on engagement drivers, frustration points, career aspirations, and flight risk indicators.
Guidelines for running peer-to-peer and manager-led recognition including daily briefing shout-outs, Wall of Wins maintenance, monthly awards, and tenure milestone celebrations.
Comprehensive annual survey covering engagement, satisfaction, culture, leadership, development, and wellbeing, with benchmarking guidance against UK hospitality sector norms.

Comprehensive first-day checklist covering right-to-work verification, contract signing, venue tour, safety briefing, team introductions, policy review, and equipment allocation. Signed by new starter and manager.
Five-shift training plan mapping specific learning objectives, observation tasks, practice activities, and buddy debriefs for each of the new starter's first five shifts.
Guide for designated buddies covering their role and responsibilities, how to give constructive feedback, common new-starter anxieties, and escalation triggers for management involvement.
Formal record of all induction elements completed with signed acknowledgements for health and safety, allergen awareness, fire procedures, and key policies. Filed in the staff record.
Short survey deployed at the one-month mark asking new starters to rate and comment on their induction experience, buddy support, information quality, and sense of welcome.
Practical assessment form scoring espresso extraction, milk steaming, drink presentation, speed, cleanliness, and customer communication. Used for baseline assessment and tier progression sign-off.
Individual training record tracking progress through Foundation, Intermediate, and Advanced tiers with module completion dates, assessor sign-offs, and notes on areas for development.
Standard operating procedures covering espresso recipe (dose, yield, time), milk steaming protocol (temperature, texture), filter brew ratios, equipment cleaning, and grinder maintenance schedules.
Template for planning weekly 30-minute training sessions including topic, learning objective, equipment needed, and follow-up practice tasks for participants.
Monthly tracker capturing extraction consistency, coffee waste volume, milk waste, customer coffee complaints, average time per drink, and correlation with training activity.
Structured review form used at 30, 60, and 90 days covering strengths, areas for improvement, agreed actions, and progressive assessment criteria. Signed by both manager and employee.
Detailed matrix defining specific, measurable assessment criteria for each probation stage — attendance, skill development, team integration, SOP compliance, and customer interaction standards.
Live tracker showing all staff currently in probation, their start date, next review date, review status, and any flags or concerns. Prevents anyone from being overlooked.

Letter templates for extending probation (with specific improvement targets and timeline) and for ending employment during probation (with reasons, notice period, and ACAS-compliant process documentation).
Quarterly analysis of probation outcomes including pass rates, extension rates, termination rates, common development areas, and correlation with recruitment source and induction quality.

Visual A3 timeline showing every touchpoint from Day -7 (pre-start) to Day 90 (probation confirmation) with milestones, responsible persons, deliverables, and cross-references to B067, B068, and B069.
Individual tracker for each new starter linking induction checklist completion, barista training tier progress, buddy feedback, and probation review outcomes in a single integrated view.
Monthly scorecard tracking time to independent competence, new starter satisfaction score, and 90-day retention rate with targets and trend analysis.
Comprehensive manager guide containing all onboarding templates, checklists, and procedures in a single reference document, ensuring consistent delivery regardless of which manager handles the hire.
Day 90 survey focused specifically on the onboarding experience — welcome, information quality, training effectiveness, buddy support, and suggestions for improvement.
Defines each career level (Barista, Senior Barista, Shift Lead, Supervisor/Manager) with role descriptions, key responsibilities, and what differentiates each level from the one below.
Detailed matrix specifying objective, assessable criteria for progression across four dimensions: technical skills, operational skills, people skills, and qualifications/training.
Self-assessment form allowing team members to evaluate themselves against the progression criteria for their target level, providing evidence and identifying development gaps.
Structured plan documenting specific actions, training, and experiences needed to meet progression criteria, with timelines, support required, and review dates.
Annual tracker monitoring progression rates, average time at each level, demographic analysis for bias detection, and correlation between progression and retention.
Structured review form covering achievements, development areas, goals for the next period, and support or training needs. Includes dual sign-off and is used for six-monthly formal reviews.
Pre-review questionnaire for team members to reflect on their achievements, challenges, skill development, and aspirations before the formal performance conversation.
Document defining observable performance standards for each key responsibility at each career level, providing objective benchmarks for review conversations and progression assessments.
Five-question guide for informal monthly check-ins covering workload, wellbeing, concerns, support needs, and any quick wins or blockers. Takes five to ten minutes per person.

Structured improvement plan for use when performance reviews identify capability concerns, defining specific targets, support measures, review dates, and consequences — aligned with ACAS capability guidance.
Grid mapping all team members against all operational skills using a four-point rating scale (Not Trained, Learning, Competent, Expert). Colour-coded to highlight single-person dependencies.
Per-person development plan identifying two to three priority skills from the matrix, specific learning actions, timelines, and support needed. Reviewed quarterly and linked to performance reviews.
Monthly calendar allocating protected training time for skill development based on matrix priorities, with assigned trainers, topics, and expected outcomes.
Rota-planning tool that cross-references the daily staff roster against the skills matrix to verify that each shift has minimum required competency coverage across all operational areas.
Quarterly report tracking the number of skill-level increases across the team, identifying top developers, persistent gaps, and correlation with operational performance metrics.
Laminated A5 card listing the five briefing points (Recognition, Priorities, Need to Know, Issues, Close) as a prompt for whoever is leading the daily huddle.

Pre-briefing preparation template with sections for recognition item, specials and allergens, staffing, expected busy periods, equipment status, and food safety reminder. Completed before the briefing and filed for records.

Guide for anyone leading the daily briefing covering the format, timing discipline (five to ten minutes), setting the right tone, handling complaints raised in the briefing, and managing quiet or disengaged groups.
Brief end-of-shift note left by the closing team for the opening team, highlighting anything the morning crew needs to know — equipment issues, stock shortages, incidents, or customer feedback.
Monthly tracker recording whether briefings occurred each day, who led them, whether briefing sheets were completed, and any themes emerging from the need-to-know or issues sections.
Timed 40-minute agenda covering action review, performance snapshot, operational update, training slot, team input, and actions close. Printable A4 format with space for notes.
Structured minutes template capturing date, attendees, absentees, key discussion points, decisions made, action items with owners and deadlines, and next meeting date.
Guide for team members rotating into the meeting chair role, covering preparation checklist, facilitation tips, time management, handling dominant voices, and encouraging quieter contributors.
Rolling tracker of all actions agreed in weekly meetings, with owner, deadline, status, and completion date. Reviewed at the start of each meeting to maintain accountability.
Quarterly anonymous survey asking team members to rate the usefulness of weekly meetings, quality of information shared, opportunity to contribute, and suggestions for improvement.
Pocket-sized reference card explaining the Situation-Behaviour-Impact feedback model with positive and constructive examples relevant to cafe operations.
Framework for co-creating a team communication charter covering agreed norms for giving feedback, raising concerns, handling disagreements, and sharing information.

Three-stage protocol for resolving workplace disagreements: direct SBI conversation, facilitated discussion with manager, and formal grievance under ACAS guidelines. Includes documentation templates for each stage.

Scenario-based guide for managers on conducting difficult feedback conversations covering underperformance, hygiene concerns, interpersonal conflict, and attendance issues. Includes scripts and preparation checklists.
Annual anonymous survey measuring psychological safety, feedback frequency and quality, information flow clarity, trust in management communication, and openness to raising concerns.

Register documenting every identified key person risk with critical function description, current sole holder, impact rating (low/medium/high), mitigation plan, backup nominee, and target resolution date.
Visual grid showing all critical functions against team members, indicating primary performer and backup coverage. Highlights single-person dependencies in red and full coverage in green.

Comprehensive pack enabling a deputy to run the cafe for two weeks including supplier contacts, equipment service numbers, bank procedures, alarm codes, utility details, insurance information, and landlord contact.
Individual development plan for each nominated deputy covering the specific skills and knowledge they need to acquire, training schedule, assessment criteria, and readiness date.
Assessment template for the annual shadow day when the owner steps away, capturing what went well, what problems arose, what could not be resolved, and what gaps need closing.
Per-person plan identifying primary station, first and second cross-training targets, assigned trainer, scheduled sessions, and target competency dates.
Practical assessment checklist for each station listing specific tasks the learner must demonstrate competently before being signed off as independently capable.
Monthly schedule template showing protected cross-training time blocks for each pair, ensuring training is planned rather than ad hoc and does not conflict with peak service periods.
Simple log recording each cross-training session with date, trainer, learner, station, tasks covered, competency level reached, and notes for next session.
Quarterly report showing coverage levels for all critical functions, percentage of team with multi-station competency, resilience test results, and priority gaps for the next training cycle.
Laminated card for the staff area showing the absence notification process: who to call, when, how, and what information to provide. Includes manager and backup contact numbers.
Ranked list of team members available for short-notice cover with typical availability by day, contact numbers, and notes on which stations they can cover.
Pre-planned contingency responses for different absence scenarios (one down, two down, key person absent) specifying which services continue, which are suspended, and customer communication scripts.
Short structured conversation guide for use after every absence, covering reason for absence, current wellbeing, any support needed, and any workplace adjustments required.
Monthly tracker capturing absence days per employee, Bradford Factor scores, short-term versus long-term split, cost of absence, pattern analysis, and benchmarking against sector averages.

Worksheet for walking through a full operating day and identifying all undocumented knowledge, organised by time of day and operational area, with space to note who currently holds each piece of knowledge.
Consistent one-page template for all Operations Manual entries with sections for title, scope, step-by-step procedure, rationale, key contacts, last updated date, and owner.
Master index of all Operations Manual entries organised by function, showing document title, section owner, last updated date, and next review date for quick navigation.
Log recording every update to the Operations Manual with date, document changed, nature of change, who made the change, and reason for the update.
Structured checklist for quarterly review of the Operations Manual covering document currency, identified gaps, new procedures needing documentation, and team feedback on usability.
One-week tracking sheet where the owner records how they spend every 30-minute block, categorised by activity type, with a final analysis showing the split between owner-only and delegatable tasks.
Two-column document clearly separating owner strategic responsibilities from manager and team operational responsibilities, shared with the entire team for transparency.
Comprehensive grid mapping every operational function to a specific role and delegation level (Do and report, Do and flag issues, Recommend for approval, Refer for decision).
Clear guide defining what constitutes a genuine owner escalation versus what should be handled independently by the manager or shift lead, with worked examples from cafe operations.

Monthly tracker monitoring the owner's time split between strategic and operational activities, with targets, trend analysis, and identification of tasks that keep pulling the owner back to the floor.
Three-category agenda template (Must Do, Should Do, Want To Do) for structuring management time sessions with space to list specific tasks and prioritise by urgency and strategic value.
Recurring weekly planner pre-allocating management time blocks to specific activities: financial review, team development, supplier management, marketing, compliance, and strategic planning.
Session-by-session log recording date, duration, planned activities, actual activities completed, and outcomes. Reviewed monthly to assess effectiveness and identify drift towards operational tasks.

Half-day agenda for quarterly off-site strategic planning covering business plan review, financial performance assessment, goal progress, priority setting for the next quarter, and strategic risk review.
Quarterly report tracking total management time hours used, strategic versus operational task split, key outcomes achieved, and correlation with business performance indicators.

Simple self-assessment covering core leadership skills (feedback, delegation, communication, conflict resolution, motivation, decision-making) rated 1-5 for confidence, with space to identify priority development areas.
Structured plan for each leader covering current strengths, priority development areas, specific learning actions (reading, courses, practice), timelines, and quarterly review dates.
Defines expected leadership behaviours at each level (shift lead, manager, owner) across core competencies, providing clear development targets and progression criteria.
Guide for running monthly 60-minute leadership learning sessions including suggested topics, discussion prompts, real-scenario exercises, and follow-up practice commitments.
Annual anonymous feedback questionnaire for direct reports, peers, and managers to assess leadership effectiveness across communication, support, fairness, development, and decision-making.

Personal assessment covering weekly hours worked, days off taken, sleep quality, energy level, relationship health, and burnout warning signs. Designed for honest self-reflection, not external reporting.
Written personal policy documenting maximum weekly hours, minimum days off per week, mandatory annual holiday allocation, and communication boundaries during off hours.
Quarterly self-assessment tracking trends in hours, rest, energy, motivation, and personal relationships over time, with early warning indicators for burnout and trigger points for seeking support.

Curated directory of support resources available to cafe owners including mental health services (Mind, Samaritans), business support (FSB, ACAS), peer networks (SCA, local groups), and coaching services.

Practical checklist confirming that all operational systems are in place to support the owner taking extended time off: delegation matrix active, key person risks mitigated, absence cover arranged, deputy briefed.
Spreadsheet template for extracting and visualising EPOS transaction data by hour and day of week, identifying peak, moderate, and quiet trading periods with clear visual patterns.
Hour-by-hour staffing requirement template showing the number of staff needed per role (barista, till, floor, kitchen) based on predicted transaction volumes for each trading period.
Comprehensive matrix defining optimal and minimum staffing levels by role and hour for each day type (quiet weekday, busy weekday, standard weekend, peak weekend, event day).

Weekly tracker comparing actual labour costs against revenue, calculating labour percentage, revenue per labour hour, and covers per staff hour with trend analysis and alerting when targets are exceeded.
Forward-looking planning tool incorporating historical data, seasonal patterns, weather forecasts, and local events to predict demand three to seven days ahead and recommend staffing adjustments.
Structured observation template for timing key service intervals during the morning rush: queue-to-order, order-to-start, start-to-handoff. Includes space for noting bottlenecks and wasted motion.

Complete standard operating procedure for morning rush service covering station assignments, communication protocols, drink sequencing, food integration, restocking triggers, and contingency responses.
Laminated cards for each rush role (Till, Bar, Support, Floater) describing that role's responsibilities, position, communication duties, and what not to do during peak service.
Weekly tracker recording average order-to-handoff times during the morning rush, peak transaction count, queue length at peak, and customer wait feedback. Used to measure workflow improvement.

Pre-planned responses for common morning rush disruptions: grinder failure, milk shortage, espresso machine fault, till crash, and staff absence. Specifies immediate action, communication to customers, and escalation path.
Comprehensive station-by-station checklist covering every item that must be in position before the morning rush: bar supplies, till setup, food display, floor readiness, and support stock levels.

Defines the exact quantities of each consumable required at each station before the rush begins: number of milk jugs filled, cups stacked by size, syrup bottles at minimum level, backup stock positioned.
Laminated photographs showing exactly how each station should look when fully prepared for the rush — bar setup, cup positioning, food display arrangement, and till station layout.

Daily record of espresso dialling-in during pre-rush prep: dose weight, yield, extraction time, taste notes, and any grinder adjustments made. Ensures the bar is calibrated before the first customer order.
Daily log recording prep start time, completion time, any gaps identified in the quality check, and correlation with rush performance. Used to optimise prep timing and staffing.

Simple A5 sheet for capturing four core peak metrics during or immediately after the morning rush: throughput count, service time samples, remakes tally, and maximum queue length with walkaway observations.
Consolidated weekly view of all peak metrics with trend lines, comparisons to previous weeks, staffing configuration notes, and space for improvement actions and their measured impact.
Template for timing each step of the drink production process during peak service to identify the bottleneck step, with space for analysing potential solutions and their estimated impact.
Running log of all peak performance improvement actions attempted, with description, implementation date, measured before and after data, outcome assessment, and decision to continue or revert.

Comprehensive quarterly analysis combining peak operational data with financial metrics, customer feedback, and team satisfaction to identify systemic improvement opportunities and investment priorities.

Graph paper or digital template for drawing the cafe's physical layout showing all equipment, counters, services, and movement paths. Includes a legend for role-specific path colours and annotation symbols.
Structured observation template for recording movement waste during a rush: instances of waiting, walking, blocking, collisions, and reversing. Includes a tally format and space for notes.
2x2 matrix tool for prioritising layout improvements by impact (high/low) and difficulty (easy/hard), with space for each identified issue, estimated cost, and recommended action.
Detailed plan for medium and high-cost layout changes including description, rationale, expected impact, estimated cost, timeline, and success measurement criteria.
Comprehensive annual review of the cafe's physical layout incorporating workflow data, peak performance correlation, team feedback, and recommendations for the coming year.

Template for mapping the reach envelope at each station, listing all items used during service and categorising them as within reach (keep), outside reach but frequently used (move closer), or rarely used (remove from station).
Laminated photographs showing the exact position of every item at each station when correctly set up, serving as the reset standard for every shift start.

Monthly audit checklist applying the five-S principles to each station: Sort (nothing unnecessary), Set in order (everything in its place), Shine (clean), Standardise (matches the standard), Sustain (maintained daily).
Assessment of working height, reach distances, bending frequency, standing surface quality, and lighting at each station, with recommendations for improvements to reduce fatigue and injury risk.
Six-monthly report comparing station design against specialty coffee best practice, tracking drinks per hour per station, error rates, and team feedback on workspace quality.

Simple card for any team member to submit a workflow improvement idea: what they observed, what they suggest changing, what impact they expect, and any risks. Can be submitted physically or digitally.

Running log of every workflow improvement idea submitted, trialled, and decided upon, with columns for idea description, submitter, trial dates, measured impact, decision (adopt/modify/abandon), and SOP update status.
Visual board design with three columns — Ideas Pipeline, Currently Testing, Recently Adopted — making the improvement cycle visible and engaging for the entire team.
30-minute meeting agenda covering: review of current experiments, outcomes of completed trials, update to SOPs for adopted changes, selection of next experiments, and pipeline review.
Comprehensive annual report quantifying the cumulative impact of all workflow improvements: time saved, waste reduced, revenue gained, customer satisfaction improved, and team engagement change.
Comprehensive A4 checklist covering Safety, Equipment, Compliance, Stock, Cleanliness, and Customer Readiness categories with tick boxes, opener name, date, time of completion, and signature.
Detailed standard operating procedure accompanying the checklist, explaining the rationale for each check, the correct procedure, what to do if a check fails, and escalation contacts.
Simple form left by the closing team for the opener highlighting equipment issues, stock shortages, incidents, deferred tasks, and anything the morning team needs to know.
Monthly tracker recording whether the opening checklist was completed and signed every day, any items missed, common issues flagged, and follow-up actions taken.

Audit template for observing an opening performed by a non-regular opener, assessing adherence to the checklist, time taken, items missed, and recommendations for refresher training or checklist updates.
Laminated A4 checklist covering all closing tasks grouped by zone: Front of House, Bar, Kitchen/Prep, Cash & Admin, and Security. Includes tick boxes, sign-off line, date, and time of completion.
Detailed Standard Operating Procedure expanding each checklist item into step-by-step instructions with role assignments, time estimates, and quality standards for each task.
Simple 1-5 rating card completed each morning by the opening team, assessing the quality of the previous night's close with space for specific issue notes.
Weekly and monthly summary of closing quality scores, completion rates, common missed tasks, and closing time trends. Used in management reviews and team meetings.
A4 template with sections for key issues, equipment status, stock alerts, customer notes, outstanding tasks, and priorities for the next shift. Signed by both outgoing and incoming shift leads.
Standard Operating Procedure defining handover timing, mandatory content fields, verbal briefing protocol, quality scoring, and escalation procedures for critical issues.
Weekly tracker recording incoming shift lead quality scores for handovers received, identifying patterns in poor handovers and linking them to specific teams or individuals.
Monthly report analysing recurring themes from handover logs — equipment issues, stock shortages, customer complaints — to identify systemic problems requiring management intervention.

Spreadsheet template for calculating par levels for every stock item based on average daily usage, delivery frequency, and safety buffer. Includes columns for item name, category, daily usage, lead time, calculated par, and review date.
Pre-populated order form listing all stock items with par levels, current stock, and calculated order quantities. Allows quick adjustment for unusual demand and generates orders by supplier.
Printable labels showing the par level for each stock item, designed to be affixed to shelves, fridges, and storage areas so any team member can instantly see whether stock needs reordering.

Monthly analysis comparing par levels against actual usage, waste, and stockout incidents. Flags items where par levels need adjustment and tracks the financial impact of stock management improvements.
Laminated A5 card listing the 15-20 items most likely to run out during service, with tick boxes for OK/Low/Out status. Designed for a 5-7 minute walkthrough of all service areas.
Reference document showing where each key item is stored in back-of-house, the standard restock quantity, and the maximum front-of-house holding to prevent counter clutter.
Weekly tracker compiling mid-service check results to identify items that consistently run low, feeding data back to par-level adjustments and ordering decisions.
Quick-reference guide giving the shift lead authority and scripts for removing items from the menu when stock runs low, including suggested alternative recommendations for customers.

Simple recording sheet placed next to the waste bin for staff to log every discarded item with quantity, estimated value, and reason (expired, over-produced, damaged, returned). Totalled daily by the closing team.
Weekly spreadsheet integrating data from par levels, mid-service checks, waste logs, and ordering records. Tracks Waste %, Stockout Incidents, and Order Accuracy with trend analysis.
Day-of-week demand profiles for key items built from 4-12 weeks of sales data, enabling predictive ordering that adjusts quantities based on expected trading patterns, weather, and events.

Monthly performance report covering waste percentage against target, stockout frequency, order accuracy, cost trends, and GP% impact. Used in management review meetings and quarterly business reviews.
Data collection sheet for recording dwell time, clearing time, and reset time for each table over a representative trading day. Used to establish baseline turnover metrics.
Written protocol defining the differentiated service approach during peak periods: clearing speed targets, bill delivery timing, floor management role, and environmental cue adjustments.

tools.tool_name: Revenue Per Seat Per Hour Tracker
tools.description: Weekly spreadsheet calculating revenue per available seat per hour across different time slots and days, identifying peak and underperforming periods for targeted improvement.
Visual floor plan overlay showing average turnover rate, dwell time, and revenue contribution for each table position, informing layout decisions and furniture investments.
Data collection sheet for recording customer volumes by 15-minute intervals, queue lengths, service times, and walkaway counts during peak periods over a representative week.
One-page reference document defining peak period times, team positions, service sequence, quality non-negotiables, and escalation triggers. Laminated and posted in the staff area.
Staffing model that maps required team members and their positions to expected demand levels during each peak period, ensuring minimum coverage is always met.
Monthly scorecard tracking peak-period customers served per hour, average transaction time, walkaway rate, quality check scores, and revenue-per-peak-hour trends.
Table-by-table audit template recording table number, size, furniture type, power access, natural light, average dwell time, and observed customer preference patterns.
Formal document defining the cafe's seating zones, furniture specifications, peak/off-peak policies, laptop worker guidelines, and accessibility provisions.
Monthly analysis of revenue generated per seating zone, revenue per square foot, and dwell time by zone. Identifies highest-performing areas and underperforming sections.
Bi-annual customer survey template covering seating comfort, availability during peak, atmosphere, noise levels, and overall satisfaction with the cafe's seating experience.
Standard Operating Procedure covering the full delivery order lifecycle: tablet management, order acceptance, kitchen/bar communication, packaging, staging, rider collection, and complaint handling.
Simple whiteboard or digital display listing all active delivery orders with order number, platform, items, status (preparing/ready/collected), and expected rider arrival time.
Quick-reference card defining when and how to adjust delivery prep times or pause delivery platforms based on in-store queue length and kitchen capacity.
Weekly dashboard tracking delivery order accuracy, average prep time, rider wait time, platform ratings, commission costs, and delivery revenue as a percentage of total turnover.
Laminated visual guide showing which container, lid, label, and bag to use for each delivery menu item, with assembly instructions and photos for consistent order packaging.
Detailed SOP covering packaging materials, assembly procedures, allergen labelling requirements, quality checks before handoff, and sustainability standards.
Spreadsheet calculating the total packaging cost per delivery order by menu item, including all components (container, lid, bag, label, cutlery, napkin), feeding into delivery menu pricing.
Test log for evaluating packaging performance under simulated delivery conditions: temperature retention, structural integrity, leak prevention, and visual presentation after 20 minutes.
Audit template for evaluating each delivery platform's performance: weekly orders, average order value, commission rate, platform rating, promotional spend, and estimated net margin.
Monthly P&L tracking delivery revenue by platform, commission costs, packaging costs, labour allocation, promotional spend, and resulting net margin with trend analysis.
Item-by-item margin calculator for the delivery menu, showing dine-in price, delivery price, ingredient cost, packaging cost, commission at various rates, and resulting GP% for each item.
Preparation template for platform commission negotiations: current volume data, competitor commission intelligence, negotiation targets, and fallback positions.

Scoring template for evaluating roaster partners across quality, consistency, price, delivery reliability, training support, equipment, sustainability, and cultural fit. Used during selection and annual review.
Formal agreement template covering pricing, delivery, quality standards, training commitment, equipment terms, exclusivity (if any), and termination provisions.
Monthly quality tracking log where each coffee delivery is cupped and scored, with notes on consistency, freshness (roast date), and any deviations from expected flavour profile.
Annual calendar of roaster touchpoints: quarterly account meetings, annual roastery visit, bi-annual menu development sessions, training sessions, and collaborative events.
Matrix categorising every supplier as Critical, Important, or Convenient based on the impact of their failure, with backup supplier details and current status for each.
Laminated quick-reference cards for each critical supply category showing: who to call, what to order, expected delivery time, cost impact, and who to inform. Designed for use under pressure.
Log recording test orders placed with backup suppliers: date, items ordered, quality assessment, delivery time, pricing, and any issues. Ensures backups are verified before they are needed.
Annual assessment evaluating each primary supplier's financial stability, delivery reliability trend, quality consistency, and any emerging risks that could trigger a switch to the backup.
Simple log for recording supplier performance incidents as they occur: date, supplier, issue description, impact on the cafe, and resolution. Used as evidence for quarterly scoring.
Quarterly assessment template scoring each supplier across Quality, Reliability, Pricing, Communication, and Service on a 1-5 scale, with trend tracking and category classification.
Agenda template for quarterly supplier review meetings: scorecard presentation, improvement targets, pricing discussion, upcoming needs, and action items.
Annual strategic view of all suppliers by category, showing primary/secondary/potential tiers, performance scores, contract terms, and recommendation for the year ahead.
Visual template mapping the value each party brings to the relationship and identifying gaps where deeper collaboration could create mutual benefit.
Written agreement framework covering supply terms, pricing, training, marketing collaboration, exclusivity, dispute resolution, and termination. Signed by both parties.
Structured agenda for bi-annual partnership review meetings: performance review, achievement celebration, next-period planning, challenge resolution, and innovation pipeline discussion.
Log tracking new product ideas, industry trends, and innovation suggestions brought by supplier partners, with evaluation status and implementation decisions.
Audit template covering all milk types used: supplier, brand, price per litre, delivery schedule, shelf life, steaming quality score, and customer demand percentage.
Scoring sheet for evaluating milk steaming performance: microfoam quality (1-5), texture smoothness, latte art holdability, taste, and sweetness balance. Used for comparing brands.
Weekly log tracking milk waste by category: expired/unused, jug waste (steamed but not served), spillage, and total. Calculates waste as a percentage of total milk purchased.
Calculator comparing the cost per serve of each alternative milk against the surcharge applied, ensuring every alternative milk drink covers its additional cost and contributes to margin.
Audit template for evaluating all bakery and food suppliers: items supplied, pricing, delivery schedule, quality assessment, food cost percentage, and improvement recommendations.
Written quality specifications for key bought-in food items defining expected standards: appearance, freshness, ingredient requirements, packaging, and delivery conditions.
Daily checklist for inspecting food deliveries against quality specifications: freshness, temperature, appearance, quantity accuracy, and packaging integrity. Non-conformances are logged.
Strategic plan showing primary and alternative suppliers for each food category, with quality scores, pricing, and recommendations for portfolio optimisation.
Visual document mapping all suppliers, their locations, what they provide, delivery routes, and identified concentration risks. Used to reveal vulnerabilities at a glance.
Register listing each supply chain risk with likelihood rating, impact assessment, current mitigation measures, and owner. Reviewed quarterly.
Laminated protocol cards for each critical supply category defining actions for 1-day, 3-day, and 1-week disruption scenarios: emergency sourcing, menu adjustments, and customer communication.
Template for the cafe's annual sourcing report: key supplier profiles, origin information, ethical certifications, sustainability initiatives, and progress against sourcing goals.
Comprehensive list of every ingredient used in the cafe with purchase price per unit (kg, litre, item), supplier, and last updated date. The reference document for all menu costing.
Per-item costing template listing every ingredient with quantity per serve, unit cost, extended cost, total cost, selling price, and GP% calculation. Includes micro-costs and packaging.
Sorted ranking of all menu items from highest to lowest GP%, with total GP in pounds, sales volume, and total profit contribution. Identifies the cafe's financial winners and drains.
Calculator showing the margin impact of delivery platform commission on each menu item: ingredient cost, packaging cost, commission at 25-35%, and resulting net margin per item.
Weekly report template: food revenue, food purchases, stock adjustment, calculated food cost %, target comparison, variance commentary, and actions required.
Spreadsheet tracking weekly food cost percentage over time with 4-week rolling average, target line, and visual chart. Highlights weeks outside the target range for investigation.
Comparison template showing theoretical food cost (from B111 menu item costing) against actual food cost, calculating the variance percentage and identifying likely causes.
Formal investigation template used when food cost exceeds target by more than 3 points: supplier pricing check, waste analysis, portioning audit, sales mix review, and corrective actions.
Weekly report: drink revenue, coffee bean cost, dairy milk cost, alternative milk cost, other drink ingredient costs, total coffee cost %, target comparison, and variance commentary.
Weekly tracker showing alternative milk usage as a percentage of total drinks, cost per alt milk serve, surcharge applied, and net margin impact versus dairy equivalent.
Weekly log of 10 randomly weighed espresso doses compared to the recipe weight, calculating average dose, variance, and estimated cost impact of any drift.
Monthly comparison of coffee beans purchased against theoretical consumption (total shots pulled x recipe dose weight), identifying unexplained gaps due to waste, staff drinks, or training.
Comprehensive audit spreadsheet listing every packaging item with supplier, unit cost, weekly volume, annual spend, and percentage of total packaging budget.
Calculator showing the total packaging cost per transaction for each service channel (dine-in, takeaway, delivery), itemising every component.
Side-by-side comparison of packaging suppliers for the top 5 highest-cost items: unit price at various volumes, quality assessment, delivery terms, and sustainability credentials.
Monthly tracking of packaging spend as a line item, with trends, percentage of revenue, reusable cup uptake, and comparison to budget and prior year.
Count sheet organised by physical storage location with columns for item name, unit of measure, actual count, expected level, and variance. Used weekly for Category A items.
Step-by-step guide for conducting accurate stock counts: blind counting protocol, unit of measure standards, partial-unit estimation, recording procedures, and filing requirements.
Investigation template for stock variances exceeding 5%: item details, expected vs actual, possible causes (waste, portioning, theft, recording error), and corrective actions.
Monthly summary of stock accuracy by category: total variance value, variance as percentage of stock, trend over time, and items requiring attention.
Laminated reference card showing the date labelling system: which labels to use, what to write, where to place labels, and the colour-coded day-dot system. Posted in each storage area.
Standard Operating Procedure covering rotation principles, labelling requirements, storage organisation, daily check procedures, expired item handling, and team responsibilities.
Simple daily checklist for the opening team to verify FIFO compliance in each storage area, flag items approaching use-by dates, and record any expired items removed.
Audit template for quarterly unannounced FIFO checks: each storage area assessed for correct rotation, labelling compliance, expired items present, and overall organisation score.
Combined weekly view showing stock count data, rotation compliance, waste, purchases, and calculated shrinkage with source breakdown.
Formal procedure for investigating stock variances above threshold: triggers, investigation steps, evidence gathering, root cause analysis, corrective actions, and follow-up.
Visual weekly dashboard showing total shrinkage value and percentage, breakdown by source (waste, portioning, recording, unknown), trend over time, and comparison to target.
Weekly log of 5 random portion weight checks during service: item, recipe weight, actual weight, variance, and cost impact. Used to identify and correct portioning drift.
Comprehensive spreadsheet showing every menu item with ingredient cost, packaging cost, target GP%, calculated minimum price, current price, competitor average, and recommended price.
Template for recording competitor prices across 5 comparable local cafes for all key menu categories: hot drinks, cold drinks, food, pastries. Updated bi-annually.
Structured checklist for the annual pricing review: cost data review, competitor analysis, customer feedback assessment, price adjustment recommendations, and implementation plan.
Protocol for conducting price sensitivity tests: which category to test, the price increase percentage, measurement period, volume tracking methodology, and profitability impact calculation.
Weekly spreadsheet recording revenue generated in each trading hour alongside staff count, calculating revenue per hour and revenue per labour hour for every time slot.
Visual grid showing days of the week and trading hours, colour-coded by revenue intensity. Identifies peak, shoulder, and trough periods at a glance for scheduling and promotional decisions.

tools.tool_name: Labour Cost Percentage by Hour Calculator
tools.description: Calculator showing staff cost per hour against revenue per hour for each time slot, highlighting hours where labour cost percentage exceeds viable thresholds.
Planning template for designing promotions targeting specific low-revenue time slots: offer design, cost, expected uplift, measurement period, and ROI calculation.
Training guide covering the philosophy of suggestive selling as hospitality, the top 5 upsell opportunities, timing guidance, customer-reading skills, and authentic phrasing examples.
Weekly tracker recording average transaction value and food attachment rate with trend chart, target line, and comparison to baseline. Displayed in the staff area.
Simple daily card identifying the featured item for suggestive selling, with a brief reason why (fresh, seasonal, needs selling, excellent today) and a suggested phrase.

Monthly analysis showing which add-on items have the highest suggestion-to-sale conversion rate, the revenue impact of each upsell type, and recommendations for focusing training on the most effective opportunities.
Strategic planning template that maps all current and potential revenue streams, identifies growth opportunities by daypart and channel, and sets phased targets for revenue expansion.
Standardised weekly reporting dashboard tracking total revenue, revenue by channel, average transaction value, covers by daypart, and progress against growth targets.
Structured tracker for managing revenue growth experiments including hypothesis, test parameters, success metrics, results, and go/no-go decisions for scaling.
