Calculator for Revenue per Available Seat Hour across different dayparts and days of the week, identifying underperforming periods with the greatest growth potential.
Standardised template for conducting the weekly financial review, covering revenue, cost of goods, payroll, cash position, VAT liability, and key financial ratios with variance indicators.
Forward-looking cash flow forecast updated weekly, showing expected inflows and outflows over the next 13 weeks with alerts for potential cash pinch points.
Calculator that determines the weekly revenue required to cover all fixed and variable costs, updated as cost base changes and showing buffer above breakeven.
Traffic light scorecard summarising the key weekly financial health indicators including food cost %, labour %, prime cost, cash position, and revenue variance against budget.

A 12-month spreadsheet template with pre-built rows for all cafe revenue streams and cost categories, including formulae for gross margin, net profit, and running cash balance. Includes a tab for assumptions and known cost changes.
Monthly one-page report comparing budgeted figures against actuals across all categories, highlighting variances exceeding 5% and documenting root causes and corrective actions.
Three-scenario budget model (pessimistic, expected, optimistic) showing the impact on monthly profit and cash balance, with trigger points for when contingency actions should be activated.
One-page weekly summary showing revenue, key cost lines, and profit against the weekly budget target. Designed for rapid management review and early identification of trends.
A one-page visual document showing how each pound of revenue is allocated across ingredients, wages, overheads, and profit. Designed to be shared with the whole team in plain language.
A structured training module covering revenue, COGS, labour cost, and profit concepts in cafe-specific terms. Includes worked examples and three actionable behaviours every team member can adopt.
A whiteboard or printed template showing daily revenue target, yesterday's actual, week-to-date position, and a motivational target for the team. Updated daily by the shift leader.
A simple 3-slide template for monthly team financial updates: how the month went vs budget, one thing that went well, and one area for improvement. Designed to be delivered in 5 minutes.

Weekly spreadsheet tracking total labour cost against revenue by day, calculating labour percentage and identifying overstaffed and understaffed periods. Includes oncost calculations for true hourly cost.

A set of shift templates for different day types (quiet weekday, busy weekday, Saturday, Sunday) specifying staffing levels by hour, roles required, and expected revenue. Used as the basis for weekly rota building.
Weekly and monthly tracker showing revenue per labour hour trend, benchmarked against target and industry averages. Highlights shifts or days with unusually low productivity for investigation.
A simple calculator for evaluating equipment or technology investments that reduce labour hours. Compares purchase cost against monthly labour savings to determine payback period in months.
Annual spreadsheet tracking all occupancy-related costs (rent, rates, service charge, insurance, maintenance) against revenue, calculating occupancy percentage monthly and identifying trends.
A calendar document flagging all critical lease dates — rent reviews, break clauses, lease expiry, insurance renewal — with 6-month advance warnings and action checklists for each milestone.
A structured document for gathering and presenting evidence at rent review negotiations, including comparable rents, property condition notes, footfall data, and any negative factors affecting value.
Calculates annual revenue per square foot of trading space and compares against industry benchmarks, highlighting whether the premises is being used efficiently.

A comprehensive template categorising all cafe costs into five groups (COGS, Labour, Occupancy, Overheads, Discretionary), calculating each as a percentage of revenue and benchmarking against industry norms.
Structured agenda for the quarterly cost review meeting, examining each cost category's current level, trend, benchmark comparison, and action items for improvement.
Tracker for the cafe's top suppliers showing annual spend, last price review date, contract terms, alternative suppliers identified, and next review date.

A structured template for evaluating whether to produce in-house or buy externally, comparing full costs (ingredients, labour, equipment, energy, waste) against wholesale purchase price and quality factors.

Monthly spreadsheet tracking gross delivery revenue, commission paid, net revenue, packaging costs, and estimated additional labour cost for each delivery platform, calculating the true effective commission rate and net margin.

A per-order calculator showing the true profitability of a delivery order after all costs: platform commission, food cost, packaging, and labour allocation. Compares against dine-in and takeaway margins.

Analyses each menu item's profitability when sold via delivery, accounting for platform commission and packaging. Identifies items that should be promoted, repriced, or removed from the delivery menu.
A structured document for preparing delivery platform commission negotiations, including current volumes, order values, review ratings, comparable rates, and specific terms being requested.

A matrix assessing every potential delivery menu item across four dimensions: margin after commission, travel durability, packaging cost, and customer satisfaction. Items are scored and ranked to determine inclusion on the delivery menu.

A worksheet for designing delivery bundles and meal deals, calculating the combined margin, packaging requirements, and target price point that increases average order value while maintaining profitability.

A card for each delivery menu item specifying preparation method, packaging type, sealing instructions, maximum hold time, and any modifications needed versus the dine-in version to ensure delivery quality.

A structured quarterly review examining each delivery menu item's sales volume, margin, customer rating, and refund rate. Includes prompts for seasonal adjustments, new item testing, and competitor monitoring.
A comparison template for evaluating direct ordering platforms across criteria including cost, POS integration, delivery logistics, branding options, customer data access, and ease of use.
Weekly tracker showing delivery and collection order volumes by channel (each platform plus direct), calculating channel mix percentages and tracking the shift toward direct ordering over time.
A set of promotional assets for driving direct ordering adoption: table card template, till receipt message, social media post templates, email announcement template, and staff talking points.
Monthly calculator showing the commission saved by orders placed through the direct channel rather than platforms, demonstrating the financial return on direct ordering investment.
A daily form recording the float denomination breakdown at the start of trade, signed by the team member who counted and verified it. Includes space for notes about any discrepancies or issues.
A detailed form for recording the end-of-day cash count by denomination, calculating total cash, subtracting the float, and comparing to POS cash revenue. Signed by the counting staff member.
A log recording every cash discrepancy with date, shift, staff on duty, amount over or short, investigation notes, and resolution. Used to identify patterns and take corrective action.

Comprehensive standard operating procedure covering all aspects of cash management: float preparation, till operation, mid-shift drops, end-of-day counting, safe management, bank deposits, and counterfeit handling.
A daily form capturing POS total revenue, cash counted, card terminal total, discrepancy amount, and closing team member signature. Designed to be completed in under 10 minutes at close of trade.
A step-by-step checklist for the entire closing procedure including POS Z-read, card terminal settlement, cash count, reconciliation, safe storage, and securing the premises.
A weekly aggregation of daily reconciliation data showing total revenue, revenue by category, cash vs card split, total discrepancies, and comparison against weekly budget target.
A log for recording and investigating discrepancies that exceed the tolerance threshold. Captures date, amount, POS transaction review findings, possible causes, actions taken, and resolution.

A spreadsheet logging every review received across all platforms: date, platform, rating, key themes, response status, response date, and any operational actions triggered. Used for monthly analysis and trend tracking.

Standard operating procedure defining the approach, tone, and structure for responding to positive, mixed, and negative reviews. Includes template structures, response timeframes, escalation triggers, and examples of good and poor responses.
A monthly report summarising total reviews received, average rating by platform, top three positive themes, top three negative themes, response compliance rate, and recommended operational actions.

A procedure for handling reviews that allege serious issues such as food poisoning, discrimination, or misconduct. Defines when to escalate to the owner, when to involve insurers or solicitors, and how to respond publicly.
A comprehensive checklist covering every element of a fully optimised Google Business Profile: basic information, categories, attributes, photos, description, menu, products, Q&A, and initial posts.
A monthly content calendar for Google Posts with suggested themes for each week (new items, behind-the-scenes, seasonal, community), image requirements, and posting schedule.
A monthly report template capturing key GBP metrics: profile views, search queries, customer actions (directions, calls, website visits), photo views vs competitors, and trends over time.
A quarterly tracker monitoring the cafe's position in local search results for key terms, tracking changes over time and correlating with GBP management activities.

A ready-to-print kit containing the cafe's Google review direct link QR code formatted for counter cards, table tents, receipt inserts, and exit door stickers. Includes the recommended review request message.
A brief training guide for staff on how to ask for reviews naturally, when to ask (and when not to), example phrases, and how to handle customers who seem interested but unsure.
A weekly tracker logging new reviews received by platform, running total, average rating trend, and comparison against the weekly target. Used to monitor review velocity and adjust strategy.
An email template sent to customers after their visit (via loyalty or ordering system) thanking them and including a direct review link. Written in a warm, non-pushy tone with the cafe's brand voice.
A structured template for conducting a comprehensive reputation audit across all platforms, recording ratings, review counts, sentiment themes, local search position, and competitor benchmarks.

A monthly visual dashboard tracking Google rating, review count, review velocity, response rate, sentiment trend, local search ranking, and competitor comparison. Designed for quick management review.

A step-by-step protocol for responding to a reputation crisis: rating drops, viral negative reviews, or coordinated complaint patterns. Covers immediate response, operational fixes, and recovery measurement.
A checklist of ways to leverage a strong reputation: marketing material updates, award applications, PR opportunities, recruitment positioning, and pricing strategy considerations.

A monthly calendar template defining posting days, content pillars for each post (Product, People, Place, Purpose), caption prompts, and hashtag sets. Designed to make content planning quick and consistent.

A curated list of hashtags organised by category (local area, coffee industry, food, lifestyle) with recommended combinations for different post types. Updated quarterly to reflect trending and effective tags.
A monthly report capturing key Instagram metrics: follower count, reach, impressions, engagement rate, top-performing posts, and audience demographics. Used to refine content strategy.

tools.tool_name: Reel and Stories Content Ideas Library
tools.description: A library of 30+ Reel and Stories content ideas specific to cafes, organised by difficulty level and time to produce. Includes latte art pours, day-in-the-life, food prep, staff introductions, and seasonal themes.

A concise training document covering the fundamentals of cafe photography: natural light usage, composition rules, food styling basics, drink photography techniques, and recommended editing apps and presets.

A reference document defining the cafe's visual identity for photography: colour palette, preferred composition styles, editing presets, examples of on-brand and off-brand images, and guidelines for different content types.
A brief template for commissioning an annual professional photo shoot, specifying shot requirements by category, styling notes, brand guidelines, and deliverable format requirements.

A system for organising, categorising, and tracking the cafe's visual content library. Categories include drinks, food, interior, exterior, team, seasonal, and user-generated. Tracks usage and freshness of each image.

A monthly analysis template for reviewing social media performance across platforms: top and bottom performing content, engagement rate trends, follower growth, and content type performance comparison.

A guide for efficiently adapting content across Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and other platforms, specifying format adjustments, optimal dimensions, caption adaptation, and platform-specific best practices.
A tracker for paid social advertising campaigns: budget spent, reach achieved, engagement generated, profile visits, website clicks, and estimated cost per potential customer reached.

A comprehensive document recording the cafe's social media best practices: optimal posting times, top content types, effective hashtag combinations, caption formulas, engagement tactics, and response protocols.

A one-page reference document showing the cafe's logo (with usage rules), primary and secondary colours (with hex codes), fonts, and a one-sentence brand personality description. Designed for quick reference by team and suppliers.
A comprehensive brand guide covering logo usage, colour palette, typography, photography style, tone of voice, and design templates. Includes examples of correct and incorrect usage for each element.

A guide defining how the cafe communicates in writing: personality traits (e.g., friendly, knowledgeable, relaxed), vocabulary preferences, example sentences for different contexts (menu, social media, signage, email), and things to avoid.

A digital folder containing all brand assets in usable formats: logo in vector (SVG, EPS) and raster (PNG, JPEG) formats on various backgrounds, colour swatches, and font files. Ready to share with any supplier or designer.

A weekly tracker recording how new customers discovered the cafe, categorised by channel (walk-past, Google, Instagram, word-of-mouth, partnership, other). Used to identify the most effective acquisition sources.
A checklist for evaluating the first-visit experience from a new customer's perspective: visibility, signage, entrance, ordering clarity, staff welcome, menu accessibility, and return incentive.
A complete referral programme package: programme rules, referral card design, staff briefing on how to distribute and explain cards, and tracking method for measuring programme effectiveness.
A template for approaching local businesses about partnership opportunities: suggested partnership formats, mutual benefits, flyer/discount card design, and follow-up tracking.
A comprehensive checklist of all customer touchpoints where brand is expressed, organised by category (physical, digital, experiential). Used as the basis for the brand consistency audit.

A scoring template for assessing each touchpoint against the brand style guide, with columns for touchpoint name, current status, consistency score, photographic evidence, and corrective action needed.
A prioritised action plan for correcting brand inconsistencies, with columns for touchpoint, issue, priority, corrective action, responsible person, budget required, deadline, and completion status.

A visual map of the customer journey showing each brand moment from arrival to departure, the intended brand expression at each stage, and the elements that create that expression (signage, service, music, presentation).

A structured document for mapping the local community: nearby businesses, organisations, schools, residential demographics, and potential partners. Includes contact information and notes on potential engagement opportunities.

A register of active community partnerships showing the partner, nature of the relationship, benefits to each party, key contact, and next review date. Used to manage and nurture community relationships systematically.

A planning template for community events: event concept, date, time, resource requirements, promotion plan, budget, expected attendance, and post-event review. Designed for repeatable use across different event types.

A tracker measuring the business impact of community engagement: revenue on event days vs. normal days, new customers acquired through community channels, media coverage received, and community group visit frequency.
A planning template for cafe events covering: concept, date, time, format, capacity, pricing, resources needed, promotion plan, budget, and post-event debrief questions.

A one-page media-ready document about the cafe: name, location, USP, owner bio, key facts, 3-4 high-resolution photos, and press contact details. Designed to make it easy for journalists to write about the cafe.
A tracker for managing collaboration partnerships: potential and active partners, collaboration concept, outreach status, terms agreed, activities completed, and impact measured.

A calendar of PR opportunities (national days, seasonal moments, local events) with planned pitches, media contacts targeted, stories submitted, and coverage received. Tracks earned media value over time.

A structured checklist for auditing the cafe's exterior from every approach direction, covering signage visibility, facade condition, window display, entrance appeal, cleanliness, and overall first impression. Includes a scoring system and photo documentation prompts.
A monthly planning template for window displays specifying the theme, key items to feature, any printed materials needed, seasonal elements, and installation schedule.

A maintenance schedule covering weekly tasks (window cleaning, A-frame update, litter sweep), monthly tasks (facade check, planter maintenance), seasonal tasks (deep clean, decoration changes), and annual tasks (signage inspection, paint assessment).

A library of 30+ A-frame board messages organised by theme (daily specials, seasonal, humorous, informative) designed to be rotated daily. Each message is concise, compelling, and aligned with the cafe's brand voice.
A comprehensive cleaning schedule organised by frequency (continuous, hourly, quiet periods, close, weekly, monthly) with specific tasks, responsible roles, and sign-off columns for accountability.
An hourly log sheet posted inside the toilet area where staff initial after completing a check and tidy. Covers cleanliness, supplies (soap, paper, hand dryer), and any issues requiring maintenance.

Standard operating procedure covering all front-of-house cleaning tasks: products used (with COSHH compliance), dilution ratios, techniques, colour-coded cloth system, safety requirements, and quality standards.
A structured checklist for the manager's weekly walk-through covering every front-of-house area, scoring each on a 1-5 cleanliness scale and noting any issues requiring attention.

A comprehensive checklist for auditing all non-customer-facing areas: kitchen, storage, fridge/freezer, cleaning cupboard, and staff area. Covers organisation, labelling, cleanliness, food safety compliance, and maintenance.
A visual map showing the designated location for every category of item in the kitchen and storage areas. Includes shelf-by-shelf labelling guides and FIFO rotation direction indicators.
Standard operating procedure covering delivery intake, stock rotation, prep area setup, clean-as-you-go requirements, end-of-shift breakdown, and weekly deep clean assignments.
A weekly inspection scorecard for the back-of-house areas, scoring organisation, cleanliness, stock rotation compliance, equipment condition, and food safety compliance on a 1-5 scale.

A comprehensive visual guide showing the ideal presentation state for every area of the cafe: exterior, entrance, counter, seating zones, food plating, drink presentation, and toilets. Each area has a reference photograph and a checklist.
A laminated card showing the exact table reset standard: what the table should look like after clearing (surface clean, items positioned, chair aligned). Kept at the clearing station for reference.

Laminated cards for the kitchen pass showing the plating standard for each menu item: plate type, portion positioning, garnish, sauce placement, and finishing details. Ensures consistency regardless of who is plating.

A monthly mystery customer assessment scoring presentation across every touchpoint on a 1-5 scale: exterior, entrance, counter, tables, food, drinks, toilets, and overall impression. Includes notes and improvement recommendations.
A template for drawing the cafe floor plan showing all seating, tables, counter, service areas, and customer flow paths. Includes a cover count calculator and seating type breakdown.

A structured observation guide for recording how customers use the space during different trading periods: which seats fill first, which are avoided, where bottlenecks occur, and how groups arrange themselves.
A weekly calculator dividing daily revenue by available covers to determine revenue per seat. Tracks this metric over time and benchmarks against industry averages and the cafe's own target.

A planning worksheet for designing distinct seating zones within the cafe, specifying the purpose of each zone, furniture type, power access, target customer, expected dwell time, and contribution to overall capacity.

A structured checklist for auditing the cafe's sensory environment across four dimensions: lighting (type, colour temperature, brightness, dark spots), music (genre, volume, appropriateness), temperature (comfort, consistency, draughts), and scent (coffee aroma, cleaning, cooking).

A schedule defining the music playlist and volume level for each daypart (morning, midday, afternoon, evening if applicable). Includes playlist names, genre guidelines, volume settings, and notes on when to adjust.

A plan documenting the cafe's lighting design: fixture types and locations, bulb specifications (wattage, colour temperature), dimmer settings by time of day, and accent lighting positions. Includes a simple floor plan with lighting zones marked.

A quarterly planning template for seasonal ambiance adjustments covering lighting changes (intensity, warmth), music playlist updates, temperature settings, and any seasonal sensory additions (candles in winter, open windows in summer, seasonal decorations).

A structured scoring matrix covering lighting, acoustics, temperature, seating comfort, aroma, visual appeal, and cleanliness. Scored 1-5 at three times of day to establish baselines and track improvements.
A comprehensive template for defining the target atmosphere profile, including sensory specifications by time of day, brand alignment notes, and reference imagery.
A twelve-month calendar template for planning seasonal visual refreshes, artwork rotations, plant replacements, and themed displays with associated budgets.
A monthly tracking dashboard that logs atmosphere scores, customer feedback themes, dwell time data, and improvement actions taken to monitor long-term atmosphere quality.

A comprehensive register template capturing equipment details including make, model, serial number, purchase date, warranty expiry, supplier, service contact, and current condition rating for every piece of cafe equipment.

A printable daily checklist covering all daily maintenance tasks including espresso machine backflushing, grinder cleaning, fridge temperature recording, dishwasher filter cleaning, and general equipment inspection.
A twelve-month calendar view showing all scheduled maintenance tasks — daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual — for every piece of equipment, with completion tracking and overdue alerts.

A quarterly assessment template for reviewing each piece of equipment's condition, maintenance history, repair frequency, and remaining useful life to inform replacement planning and budget allocation.

Laminated A4 checklist covering end-of-day machine cleaning: backflush with detergent, group head purge, steam wand clean, drip tray empty, and exterior wipe. Signed off daily by closing barista and countersigned by manager.

Comprehensive log recording all maintenance activity: daily cleaning sign-offs, parts replacements, engineer visits, water filter changes, and any faults reported. Forms the auditable maintenance history for warranty and insurance purposes.
Four-tier maintenance calendar specifying daily, weekly, monthly, and annual tasks with responsible roles, expected time per task, and parts or consumables required.

Visual guide covering common espresso machine faults such as low pressure, no water flow, steam issues, and error codes, with step-by-step resolution for minor issues and clear escalation criteria for engineer callouts.
Weekly tracking of brew temperature, pump pressure, flow rate, and shot count. Visualises trends to detect gradual degradation before it affects cup quality or causes breakdown.

Comprehensive spreadsheet listing every major equipment item with manufacturer, model, serial number, purchase date, cost, expected lifespan, condition score, warranty status, and service history. The single source of truth for all equipment data.

Forward-looking capital expenditure plan showing which equipment items are due for replacement in each year, estimated costs with inflation adjustment, and cumulative funding requirements. Used for financial planning and cash flow management.

Calculator that takes the 5-year replacement forecast total and derives the required monthly savings contribution, with adjustments for existing reserves, planned financing, and priority weighting of critical vs non-critical equipment.

Standardised 6-monthly assessment form for each major equipment item covering: physical condition, performance quality, reliability, safety compliance, energy efficiency, and overall condition score (1-5). Triggers replacement planning when scores drop below threshold.

Reference document defining minimum specifications, preferred suppliers, warranty requirements, and installation standards for each major equipment category. Prevents panic-buying and ensures consistent quality across replacements.

Systematic checklist for auditing every product entry in the POS: verifying name accuracy, current pricing, correct category, complete modifiers, appropriate tax rate assignment, and active/inactive status.

Comprehensive reference document covering all POS procedures: opening setup, transaction processing, voids and refunds, discount application, split bills, end-of-day cashing up, and report generation. Includes screenshots and step-by-step instructions.
Automated report configuration showing total revenue, transaction count, average transaction value, sales by category and hour, and week-on-week comparison. Sent daily to owner and manager.

Practical assessment checklist testing each staff member on core POS operations: standard sale, modifier application, discount, void, refund, split bill, gift card, and end-of-day report. Must be completed before solo till operation.

Structured template for weekly management review of POS data covering: top and bottom sellers, day-part analysis, average transaction value trends, promotion impact, and action items for the coming week.

Assessment checklist covering kitchen capacity, POS compatibility, physical space, customer demand, and staff readiness for digital ordering implementation. Identifies blockers before any platform is selected.

Spreadsheet comparing the true cost and projected revenue of each digital ordering channel: platform fees, commission rates, packaging costs, staff time, and expected order volume. Enables informed channel selection.

Step-by-step operational procedures for handling digital orders: receiving notifications, kitchen communication, preparation prioritisation, staging for collection, customer notification, and troubleshooting common issues.

Structured template for designing the digital menu including item selection criteria, photography requirements, description standards, bundle/upsell strategy, and pricing adjustments for delivery versus collection.

Weekly report tracking digital ordering KPIs: order volume by channel, revenue, average order value, fulfilment time, error rate, customer ratings, and commission costs. Enables data-driven channel management.

Spreadsheet template for cataloguing every technology system in use: product name, category, monthly cost, contract end date, integration status, effectiveness score (1-5), and notes. Provides a complete view of the current technology stack and spend.

Structured decision framework for evaluating any new technology purchase. Covers: business problem definition, integration requirements, total 3-year cost of ownership, trial availability, data portability, training needs, and ROI projection.
Quarterly planning document specifying technology priorities, selected solutions, implementation timelines, budget allocation, responsible owners, and success metrics for each planned investment.
Monthly tracker of all technology subscriptions with renewal dates, costs, and a flag for contracts approaching renewal. Prevents unexpected charges and enables proactive negotiation or cancellation.

Secure document listing all technology systems, login credentials, admin access details, configuration notes, and vendor contact information. Ensures business continuity if the primary technology manager is unavailable.

Single-page visual dashboard showing 8 core metrics: daily revenue, transaction count, average transaction value, revenue by category, revenue by hour, day-on-day comparison, week-to-date total, and week-on-week trend. Updates daily with minimal manual input.

Structured 15-20 minute meeting agenda covering: review of last week's actions, dashboard trend analysis, identification of 2-3 priorities for the coming week, and documentation of agreed actions with owners and deadlines.

One-page monthly report aggregating dashboard data: total revenue versus target, transaction and ATV trends, category performance analysis, best and worst products, actions taken with measured outcomes, and priorities for the coming month.

Decision framework defining threshold triggers for each dashboard metric and the corresponding investigation steps and response actions. Transforms data observation into systematic business improvement.

Historical pattern-based forecasting model that predicts expected daily revenue based on day of week, month, and seasonal trends. Enables comparison of actual versus expected performance to identify genuine variances.

Systematic audit of all existing customer data sources: POS records, online ordering, Wi-Fi logins, loyalty programmes, social media, and email enquiries. Records data format, volume, consent status, and accessibility for each source.

Complete sign-up package including: counter card design, tablet sign-up form, GDPR consent wording, staff talking points, welcome email template, and sign-up incentive details. Everything needed to start collecting customer data compliantly.

12-month email marketing calendar specifying: send frequency, content themes, seasonal campaigns, promotional offers, and performance targets. Includes content templates for common email types to reduce production time.

Monthly tracking of customer lifecycle metrics: new sign-ups, active customers (engaged in last 30 days), at-risk (30-60 days inactive), lapsed (60+ days inactive), and win-back success rate. Visualises the health of the customer base.

Monthly report tracking loyalty programme metrics: total members, active participation rate, average visits per member, redemption rate, incremental revenue attributed to loyalty, and programme cost. Measures whether the loyalty programme is genuinely driving retention.

Design template for a staff-facing metrics display showing daily revenue versus target, average transaction value, customer count, weekly goal metric, and a fun engagement metric. Includes guidance on updating frequency and framing numbers with context.

Complete training materials for a 30-60 minute staff workshop covering: what key metrics mean, how daily actions influence metrics, how to read the Team Metrics Board, and practical examples of data-informed decisions relevant to cafe operations.

Simple one-page template for setting up improvement experiments: hypothesis statement, baseline measurement, proposed change, measurement period, success criterion, actual result, and decision (adopt, modify, or abandon).

Monthly log documenting instances where team members used data to make better decisions, identify problems, or suggest improvements. Used for public recognition and to build a library of data-informed decision examples for training.

Structured 30-minute agenda for quarterly all-team reviews covering: quarter performance summary, key wins and challenges, team-contributed ideas, upcoming priorities, and Q&A. Designed to be accessible and engaging for all staff levels.

Structured template for documenting all personal data processing: data type, source, purpose, lawful basis, storage location, access controls, retention period, third-party sharing, and data subject category (customer, staff, supplier).

Plain-English privacy notice template tailored for independent cafes, covering all required UK GDPR disclosures: identity, data collected, purposes, lawful bases, recipients, retention, rights, and complaint procedures. Ready for customisation and publication.

Step-by-step procedure for handling personal data breaches: identification, containment, assessment of severity, ICO notification (within 72 hours if required), individual notification, documentation, and post-incident review.

Concise training materials covering: what personal data is, staff responsibilities when handling data, marketing consent rules, CCTV awareness, reporting concerns, and practical dos and don'ts. Designed for a 20-minute training session.

Comprehensive annual checklist reviewing: ICO registration status, privacy notice accuracy, consent mechanisms, data retention compliance, third-party agreements, staff training records, breach log, SAR log, and CCTV signage.

Comprehensive audit template covering: internet provider details, speed test results, router specifications, Wi-Fi coverage map, all connected devices, network configuration, known issues, and contract/warranty dates.

Laminated quick-reference card posted near the POS with step-by-step instructions for: switching POS to offline mode, activating backup connectivity, communicating with customers, and contacting support providers.

Document listing all critical business data, where it is stored, backup method (cloud provider auto-backup vs manual vs automated script), backup frequency, last verified restoration date, and responsible person.

Comprehensive plan covering 5 critical failure scenarios (internet outage, POS failure, power loss, data loss, cyber incident) with immediate response steps, communication procedures, workaround solutions, recovery timelines, and responsible individuals.

Quarterly security checklist covering: password audit for all network equipment, firmware update status, connected device review, guest Wi-Fi password rotation, former staff access removal, and unusual activity check.

Comprehensive audit of all business online accounts covering: account name, purpose, who has access, password strength rating, MFA enabled (yes/no), last password change date, and action required. Used for initial security hardening and quarterly reviews.

Training materials for a 20-30 minute staff session covering: phishing recognition, safe payment card handling, password rules, suspicious activity reporting, and real-world examples of cafe-relevant cyber threats.

Physical security inspection checklist for card payment terminals: checking for loose components, unfamiliar attachments, broken seals, unusual cables, and software version verification. Completed monthly and after any unattended period.

Structured response procedures for 5 cyber threat scenarios: account compromise, terminal tampering, ransomware, data breach, and email fraud. Each scenario includes containment steps, contact list, communication templates, and recovery procedures.

Quarterly review covering: software updates on all systems, account access audit, MFA verification, payment terminal physical inspection, password manager review, former staff access removal, and emerging threat awareness check.

Single-page template capturing the business essence: purpose statement, target customer, key differentiator, current revenue, 12-month target, 3 strategic priorities, and 3-year vision. Designed for wall display and daily reference.

9-section business plan template tailored for independent cafes: executive summary, vision and mission, market analysis, products and services, marketing strategy, operations plan, financial plan, risk assessment, and key milestones.

Monthly financial projection spreadsheet covering: revenue by category, cost of goods sold, labour costs, overheads, profit forecast, and cash flow. Includes scenario modelling for 10% and 20% revenue shortfalls.

Register documenting the top 5-10 strategic risks with: risk description, likelihood (high/medium/low), potential impact, current mitigation measures, additional actions required, and responsible owner. Reviewed quarterly.

Structured agenda for quarterly strategic reviews covering: performance versus targets, milestone achievement, financial variance analysis, risk register updates, market changes, and priority setting for the next quarter.

Structured template for documenting each competitor: name, location, distance, price level, quality assessment, opening hours, target audience, online ratings, menu highlights, strengths, weaknesses, and competitive threat level.

One-page profile template for each customer segment covering: segment name, demographic description, visit pattern (when, how often), typical order, average spend, primary motivations, values, pain points, and estimated revenue contribution.
Visual 2x2 grid template with Price on X axis and Quality on Y axis for plotting the cafe and all competitors. Includes guidance on interpreting market gaps, crowded positions, and strategic options.

Guided workshop format for developing the cafe's positioning statement. Includes the structured sentence format, worked examples, common pitfalls, and a testing checklist to validate the statement against customer reality.

Simple 5-question customer survey designed for cafe environments: what brings you here, how often do you visit, what do you value most, what could we improve, would you recommend us. Includes distribution guidance and results analysis framework.

5-dimension self-assessment covering financial health, operational maturity, management depth, systems documentation, and market validation. Each dimension scored 1-5 with any score below 3 flagged as a blocking issue. Provides an objective readiness assessment.

Comprehensive financial model covering: capital requirements, funding sources, 24-month cash flow projection for new site, impact on existing site, break-even analysis, and stress test at 60% revenue scenario. Designed for professional review.

Structured evaluation framework for assessing potential expansion locations: footfall data, demographic analysis, competition density, lease terms, fit-out costs, licensing requirements, and proximity to existing operations.

Sequential checklist of milestones that must be completed before committing to expansion: operations manual, management readiness, financial approval, legal review, planning permissions, funding secured, and contingency confirmed.

Weekly KPI tracker for the new site covering: revenue versus projection, customer count, average transaction value, staff costs, cash flow, and comparison of existing site performance. Includes escalation triggers and decision points.

Simple reflection template capturing: 3 things that went well, 3 things that went poorly, 3 things learned, 1 thing to do differently. Completed by owner and key team members before the planning day to provide raw material for discussion.

Structured full-day agenda covering: morning review session (finances, operations, team, market, honest reflections) and afternoon planning session (priority setting, target definition, calendar planning, risk identification). Includes timing and facilitation guidance.

2-3 page document template capturing: strategic priorities with measurable targets, quarterly milestones, financial targets, top risks with mitigations, and key dates. Designed for clarity and actionability, not length.

90-minute structured agenda for quarterly progress reviews: priority-by-priority milestone review, celebration of progress, blocker diagnosis, corrective actions, and next quarter priorities. Keeps strategic thinking alive between annual planning events.

Simple tracking sheet for each strategic priority showing: priority description, measurable target, quarterly milestones, current status (on track/at risk/behind), actions required, and responsible owner. Updated monthly and reviewed quarterly.

One-page catering menu template with item descriptions, pricing, minimum order value, lead time requirements, delivery information, and ordering contact details. Designed for professional presentation via email and print.

Spreadsheet calculator for pricing each catering item: ingredient cost breakdown, labour allocation, packaging cost, delivery cost allocation, target margin, and final selling price. Ensures consistent, profitable pricing across the menu.

Combined enquiry capture form (date, guests, dietary requirements, budget, location) and quotation template with itemised pricing, delivery charges, terms and conditions, and confirmation instructions.

Step-by-step operational checklist for each catering order: ingredient ordering, production scheduling, preparation, quality check, packaging, labelling, delivery logistics, client handover, and post-delivery follow-up.
Monthly tracking of catering metrics: total revenue, order count, average order value, food cost percentage, delivery costs, net profit margin, repeat customer rate, and pipeline of upcoming orders.

Assessment checklist evaluating retail readiness: customer base analysis, product-market fit, display space availability, roaster retail support, competitor retail offerings, and initial product selection with rationale.

Spreadsheet model for pricing each retail product: ingredient or product cost, packaging cost, labour allocation, display space cost, wastage allowance, target margin, and competitive benchmark price. Ensures all retail items are profitably priced.
Guide defining retail display standards: product placement, signage requirements, lighting, stock rotation rules, seasonal refresh schedule, and photography standards for social media promotion.

Stock tracking spreadsheet for all retail products: current stock level, reorder point, reorder quantity, supplier lead time, roast date tracking (for coffee), and sell-through rate. Prevents stockouts and staleness.

Monthly report tracking: total retail revenue, retail as percentage of total revenue, units sold by product, gross margin by product, retail attachment rate, and seasonal comparison. Informs range decisions and identifies top performers.

Structured assessment covering: evening foot traffic observations, competitor mapping, lease and planning review, customer survey results, operational capacity analysis, and financial impact projection. Produces a viability score and recommendation.

Complete pilot plan covering: chosen evenings, proposition definition, menu, staffing plan, ambiance changes, break-even calculation, marketing approach, success metrics, and evaluation timeline. Designed for 4-8 week structured testing.

Spreadsheet calculating the minimum evening revenue needed to cover additional costs: staff wages, food cost of goods sold, additional utilities, licensing fees, and other marginal costs. Produces a per-evening break-even target.

Post-pilot evaluation covering: revenue versus break-even, customer count trend, average spend, customer feedback summary, staff impact assessment, operational challenges, and clear go/no-go recommendation with conditions.
Monthly event planning template for evening programming: recurring events, special events, required resources, marketing timeline, booking management, and revenue targets per event type.

Spreadsheet modelling subscription economics across multiple scenarios: price point, marginal drink cost, average redemptions, food attachment revenue, current spend displacement, and net monthly value per subscriber. Validates viability before launch.

Comprehensive design document covering: pricing rationale, drink inclusions and exclusions, redemption limits, payment method, member identification, terms and conditions, cancellation and pause policy, and staff procedures.

Pilot plan for testing the subscription model with 20-30 early adopters: recruitment approach, promotional pricing, feedback collection schedule, success metrics, evaluation criteria, and decision framework for wider launch.

Monthly communication plan for subscribers: welcome sequence, usage summaries, exclusive offers, event invitations, feedback requests, and renewal reminders. Designed to build community and reduce churn.

Monthly dashboard tracking: active subscribers, MRR, average redemptions, food attachment rate, churn rate, new subscriber acquisition, subscriber LTV, and comparison to non-subscriber customer value.

Guided worksheet with 6 reflective questions that surface the owner's authentic values. Includes instructions for pattern identification and value naming. Designed for honest, private reflection before any team involvement.

Single-page visual template listing 3-5 core values with a one-sentence meaning and 3-5 specific behavioural examples for each. Designed for wall display in the staff area with professional, on-brand presentation.

Interview question bank with 2-3 behavioural questions per core value. Each question includes: the question text, what a strong answer sounds like, what a weak answer sounds like, and scoring guidance for consistent evaluation.

Workshop facilitation guide for a 45-60 minute team session introducing the cafe's values. Includes: agenda, discussion prompts, activity for generating team-specific behavioural examples, and collective commitment exercise.
Short anonymous survey asking staff to rate how well the cafe lives each value (1-5) with space for comments. Includes analysis framework for identifying values gaps and creating action plans.

Complete handbook template with pre-structured sections for company overview, employment terms, conduct standards, absence policies, health and safety, disciplinary and grievance procedures, and data protection. Written in plain English with cafe-specific examples.
One-page sign-off form for each team member confirming receipt, reading, and understanding of the staff handbook. Includes date, printed name, signature, and a space for any questions raised.

Step-by-step disciplinary procedure following the ACAS Code of Practice, covering informal discussion, first written warning, final written warning, and dismissal. Includes a parallel grievance procedure for staff to raise formal concerns.
Laminated A4 card for display in the staff area summarising the five most frequently referenced policies: absence reporting, holiday booking, dress code, social media, and disciplinary stages.

Yearly audit checklist ensuring every handbook section is reviewed against current UK employment law, updated for any business changes, and reissued to all staff with fresh acknowledgement signatures.

Reference document defining specific toxic behaviours in cafe context — bullying, exclusion, passive aggression, gossip, undermining, favouritism — with concrete examples of what each looks like in practice and how it differs from normal workplace friction.

Four-stage intervention framework: informal conversation, formal meeting with written notes, written warning (ACAS-compliant), and final warning or dismissal. Each stage includes scripts, documentation requirements, and escalation triggers.

Practical guide for structuring behavioural conversations: opening the discussion, describing specific behaviour and impact, stating expected change, agreeing a review date, and documenting the outcome. Includes example scripts for common cafe scenarios.

Confidential record form for each intervention conversation capturing date, individuals involved, behaviour discussed, impact described, agreed actions, review date, and follow-up outcome. Filed securely and separately from general personnel records.

Anonymous survey specifically targeting interpersonal dynamics: do you feel included, respected, and psychologically safe? Are there behaviours that make you uncomfortable? Would you recommend this workplace to a friend? Tracks trends over time.

Structured interview guide with open questions exploring team members' perceptions of current culture, sources of pride, frustrations, and aspirations. Designed for individual 20-minute conversations with verbatim capture of responses.

Five-dimension measurement tool tracking pride, belonging, growth, voice, and energy on a 1-5 scale. Includes quarter-on-quarter trend charts and space for co-created action plans targeting the lowest-scoring dimension each quarter.

Monthly planning template for the rotating Culture Champion, with a menu of initiative ideas (team playlist, skills swap, social event, new tradition), a simple budget guide, and a reflection section for capturing what worked.

Supplementary onboarding guide focused on cultural immersion rather than operational training. Covers the cafe's story, values in practice, team traditions, Culture Buddy introduction, and the Culture Aspiration Statement. Designed for the first two weeks.

Comprehensive annual review framework combining scorecard trend analysis, observational assessment, customer feedback themes, external benchmarking, and Culture Aspiration Statement review. Produces a Culture Health Report with priorities for the year ahead.

Five guided questions helping the owner articulate their purpose, customer experience vision, non-negotiable standards, long-term ambition, and proudest potential outcome. Designed for 30-60 minutes of uninterrupted reflection.

Structured template for distilling the vision discovery into three sections — Our Story, Our Promise, Our Ambition — using personal, conversational language rather than corporate jargon. Designed for display in the staff area.

tools.tool_name: Monthly State of the Cafe Briefing Template
tools.description: Ten-minute briefing structure covering business performance highlights, current challenges, upcoming changes, vision connection points, and an open feedback question. Designed to be delivered verbally with minimal preparation.

Quarterly facilitation guide for reviewing the Vision Statement with the team. Includes prompts for discussing reality versus aspiration, identifying gaps, celebrating alignment examples, and co-creating action plans to close gaps.
Simple 60-second daily close-out protocol where the shift leader names one standout moment from the shift. Includes prompts for what to recognise and examples of specific, behaviour-linked praise.
Instructions for creating and maintaining a visible recognition board in the staff area. Includes seeding content ideas, maintenance schedule, and tips for encouraging team contributions.
Complete award framework including nomination criteria linked to each cafe value, a simple selection process, certificate template, reward options guide, and announcement script for team meetings.
Printed card templates with prompts for specific recognition: 'I want to thank [name] for [specific action] because [impact it had].' Designed to be displayed on the Wins Wall.

Milestone recognition framework with suggested gestures for six months (handwritten card), one year (meaningful gift and public acknowledgement), and two years (team celebration and development opportunity). Includes budget guidance for small cafes.

Structured worksheet for documenting every community connection point within walking distance of the cafe: organisations, businesses, groups, schools, and gathering places. Includes columns for potential partnership type and mutual value proposition.

Rolling register of all active community partnerships documenting the organisation, contact person, what was agreed, value flowing each direction, start date, and review date. Used to manage and nurture relationships systematically.

A5 card template for displaying alongside locally sourced products. Includes the producer's name, location, their story, what they supply, and why the cafe chose them. Designed to be visible to customers and create connection.

Event planning template covering event concept, target audience, community partner involvement, logistics, promotion plan, budget, and post-event review. Includes a menu of proven cafe event ideas to draw from.

One-page annual summary documenting the cafe's community contributions: local sourcing spend, number of events hosted, partnerships maintained, charitable contributions, and community feedback highlights. Designed for display in-store and on social media.

Comprehensive opening preparation checklist organised into four categories — food prep, beverage prep, station setup, housekeeping — with each task showing estimated time, responsible role, and completion deadline. Designed for lamination and daily sign-off.

End-of-day form completed by the closing shift communicating to the opening team: what was prepped ahead, what ran out, equipment issues, expected deliveries, and any other priorities. Ensures continuity between shifts.
Reference table linking expected daily covers to preparation quantities for all menu items requiring advance prep. Updated weekly based on actual sales data to reduce waste and prevent shortfalls.

Visual reference document with photographs showing exactly what a fully prepped station looks like for each role — bar, kitchen, display, till area. Removes ambiguity and provides a clear standard for new and experienced staff alike.

Five-minute weekly review form tracking: was the prep list completed on time each day, were there any shortfalls that affected service, and what adjustments are needed for the following week. Drives continuous improvement of the prep system.

Single A4 checklist covering daily maintenance tasks for every critical piece of cafe equipment — espresso machine, grinder, dishwasher, fridges, and ancillary machines. Includes task description, responsible role, and daily sign-off space.

Master reference document for each piece of equipment recording make, model, serial number, purchase date, supplier, warranty expiry, service contract details, engineer contact information, and a rolling log of all maintenance and repair history.

Calendar-format schedule covering all periodic maintenance tasks across all equipment: weekly deep cleans, monthly descaling, quarterly professional servicing, and annual inspections. Includes task owner, estimated time, and completion sign-off.

Simple fault reporting form kept near main equipment where any team member can log an issue: equipment name, date, fault description, severity (minor/moderate/urgent), and resolution notes. Reviewed daily by the manager.

Long-term planning tool tracking each piece of equipment by age, expected lifespan, current condition, fault frequency, and estimated replacement cost. Generates a rolling three-year capital expenditure forecast to prevent emergency purchases.
A4 printable log with columns for date, time, item, category, quantity, unit cost, total cost, reason, and staff initials. Stationed at each waste point for real-time logging throughout every shift.
Laminated quick-reference card defining each waste category with examples, placed next to waste log stations to ensure consistent categorisation across all staff.
Weekly summary aggregating daily waste logs into category breakdowns, shift-level analysis, waste-to-revenue ratios, and comparison against targets and previous periods.
Standard Operating Procedure covering waste logging methods, reconciliation against purchasing and sales data, escalation triggers for excessive waste, and monthly review protocols.
Visual dashboard showing waste-to-revenue trends, category breakdowns, shift comparisons, and progress against reduction targets. Used in monthly management reviews.
A3 printable template displaying seven core KPIs with targets, actuals, variance, and green/amber/red status indicators. Completed daily by the closing manager.
Reference document defining each dashboard metric, its calculation method, data source, target-setting rationale, and threshold definitions for green/amber/red status.
Weekly roll-up of daily dashboards showing 7-day trends, week-on-week comparisons, and highlight commentary on significant variances.
Structured 5-minute morning briefing format using yesterday's dashboard: one win to celebrate, one metric to focus on, and one action for today.
Monthly review template for assessing and adjusting dashboard targets based on actual performance trends, seasonality, and growth plans.

Comprehensive risk assessment template pre-populated with common cafe hazards organised by area (bar, kitchen, storage, customer area, exterior), with columns for hazard, who is at risk, existing controls, risk rating, additional controls needed, responsible person, and review date.

Simple one-page form for recording near-miss incidents: date, time, location, description of what happened, what prevented harm, and suggested improvement. Anonymity option included to encourage reporting.

tools.tool_name: Health and Safety SOP Library Index
tools.description: Master index of all health and safety SOPs covering burns prevention, manual handling, slip prevention, COSHH, electrical safety, and lone working, with version control and review schedule.
Register of all hazardous substances used in the cafe (cleaning chemicals, descalers, sanitisers) with safety data sheets, storage requirements, PPE requirements, and first aid measures.
Quarterly review checklist covering all risk assessment entries, near-miss trends, accident book review, control measure effectiveness, and actions arising. Signed off by owner.

Comprehensive fire risk assessment template pre-populated with common cafe fire hazards, covering ignition sources, fuel sources, people at risk, existing controls, and additional measures needed. Aligned with Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005 requirements.

Clear A4 evacuation procedure document defining roles (fire warden, caller, sweep), evacuation route, assembly point, and re-entry protocol. Includes a customisable floor plan showing exits and equipment locations.

Structured log book recording weekly alarm tests, monthly emergency lighting checks, annual extinguisher servicing, fire drill records, and any fire safety issues. Provides an auditable compliance trail.
Individual training record documenting each staff member's fire safety induction, extinguisher training, evacuation drill participation, and refresher dates.

tools.tool_name: Fire Drill Record and Debrief Template
tools.description: Template for recording fire drill date, scenario, evacuation time, issues identified, and corrective actions. Includes a debrief section for capturing lessons learned.

Comprehensive checklist covering all licensing and regulatory requirements for a cafe: food business registration, music licensing, alcohol licensing, pavement licence, employer liability insurance, data protection registration, and signage requirements.
Per-employee checklist verifying: written contract issued, right-to-work check completed, correct NMW/NLW rate, itemised payslips, pension auto-enrolment, and working time compliance.
Annual calendar tracking all licence renewal dates, insurance expiry, pension re-enrolment dates, and regulatory filing deadlines with advance warning alerts.
Cafe-specific employment contract template including all mandatory particulars required by the Employment Rights Act 1996 (as amended), customisable for different roles and working patterns.

Step-by-step onboarding checklist ensuring all legal requirements are met before or on day one: right-to-work check, contract issuance, pension information, health and safety briefing, and data protection notice.
Single-page visual summary showing compliance status across all regulatory areas with green/amber/red ratings and key dates.
Role description for the compliance champion including responsibilities, time allocation, authority level, and reporting requirements.
Step-by-step protocol for when a regulatory inspector arrives: greeting procedure, documentation to present, areas to show, questions to expect, and post-visit follow-up actions.
Comprehensive monthly checklist covering critical compliance items across all regulatory areas, completed by the compliance champion and reviewed by the owner.
Annual review template covering compliance performance, audit results, inspection outcomes, gaps addressed, and improvement targets for the coming year.

Structured scorecard covering product quality, service delivery, visual presentation, and operational standards with clear scoring criteria (pass/marginal/fail) for each element. Used during formal site audits.
Detailed brief for mystery shoppers defining the visit scenario, items to order, observations to make, service interactions to assess, and scoring methodology.
Structured report template for mystery shop visits covering all brand standard categories, individual scores, narrative commentary, and photographic evidence where appropriate.
Template for documenting corrective actions when a site falls below brand standards: issues identified, root causes, actions to take, responsible persons, and deadlines for resolution.
Quarterly dashboard showing brand audit and mystery shop scores across all locations, trend analysis, best and worst performers, and actions arising.

Standardised SOP template with fields for title, version, date, author, purpose, scope, step-by-step instructions, quality checkpoints, equipment needed, and sign-off. Ensures consistency across all procedures.
Checklist for area managers to verify SOP adherence during site visits, covering key procedures with pass/fail scoring and space for corrective action notes.
Form for any team member to propose SOP changes: current procedure, proposed change, rationale, and expected impact. Routed through approval workflow before implementation.
Master index of all SOPs in the library with title, category, version number, last review date, next review date, owner, and status. The single reference point for the entire SOP estate.
Quarterly report summarising SOP compliance rates across locations, changes made, new SOPs issued, and upcoming reviews. Used in operations leadership meetings.
Monthly dashboard comparing all core financial metrics across every location, with estate averages, variance highlighting, and trend indicators.
Reference document defining each financial benchmark, its calculation methodology, data sources, and the standardised chart of accounts to ensure consistency across sites.
Template for investigating and reporting on significant financial variances at individual sites: metric affected, size of variance, root cause analysis, and recommended actions.
Quarterly report showing rolling trends across all benchmarks, seasonal adjustments, and commentary on structural changes affecting performance.
Annual strategic review of cross-site financial performance including external benchmarking, investment recommendations, and target-setting for the coming year.
Gantt-style project timeline showing all five phases of a new site opening, with task dependencies, milestones, decision gates, and critical path highlighted.
Detailed checklists for each opening phase covering every task, responsible person, due date, and completion status. Ensures nothing is missed during the opening process.
Comprehensive compliance checklist covering all regulatory requirements that must be met before opening day: food registration, fire safety, H&S, licensing, insurance, and certificates.
Detailed budget template covering all new site opening costs: lease, fit-out, equipment, recruitment, training, marketing, and working capital, with contingency allowances.
Structured template for the 30-day post-opening review: what went well, what went badly, what to change, timeline adherence, budget variance, and recommended playbook updates.
Comprehensive role description covering responsibilities, KPIs, reporting lines, span of control, time allocation expectations, and key competencies required for the area manager position.
Structured report template completed after every site visit covering: walk-through observations, team conversations, data review findings, actions agreed, and follow-up deadlines.
Monthly dashboard showing each area manager's performance across all KPIs: brand audit scores, financial performance, staff metrics, compliance rates, and customer satisfaction.
Structured induction checklist for new area managers covering brand standards, SOP library, financial systems, people management processes, compliance requirements, and key contacts.
Annual 360-degree feedback form collecting structured input from site managers, peers, central team, and the operations director on the area manager's performance across key competencies.
Comprehensive scorecard covering all franchise agreement obligations organised into brand, operations, financial, and legal categories with clear scoring criteria for each item.
Template for documenting non-compliance findings, assigned tier level, required corrective actions, deadlines, and escalation pathway if issues are not resolved.
Dashboard tracking compliance scores, financial performance, customer metrics, and trend data across all franchise locations.
Structured support plan for franchisees who need additional assistance: areas of concern, support actions (training, mentoring, operational assistance), milestones, and review dates.
Annual review template combining compliance data, financial performance, customer metrics, and franchisee satisfaction into a comprehensive relationship assessment.
Template for auditing current procurement across all sites: supplier details, products, prices, terms, delivery frequency, and total spend by category.
Master list of approved suppliers with approval status, product categories, pricing, terms, food safety accreditation, and review dates.
Quarterly scorecard assessing each key supplier on price competitiveness, product quality, delivery reliability, invoice accuracy, and responsiveness.
Dashboard tracking total procurement spend by category, price trends, savings versus pre-consolidation baseline, and cost as percentage of revenue across all sites.
Template for negotiating and documenting central supply agreements: volumes, pricing tiers, payment terms, delivery schedule, quality standards, and termination clauses.
Simple form for submitting a best practice: what the practice is, what problem it solves, the results observed, how to implement it, and any resources needed.
Quarterly newsletter or document compiling the best shared practices from across the network, with implementation guidance and contact details for the originating site.
Master index of all validated best practices organised by topic, with status (submitted/validated/adopted), originating site, adoption rate, and measured impact.
Structured agenda for quarterly best practice forums: featured practices, implementation updates, challenge discussions, and action planning.
Dashboard tracking the operational and financial impact of adopted best practices across the network: practices adopted, sites implementing, measured improvements, and estimated value created.

Walk-through audit template covering energy use, waste streams, water consumption, single-use items, supplier packaging, and sourcing certifications. Produces a scored baseline against which future progress is measured.
Weekly log tracking waste by stream (general, recycling, compost, coffee grounds) in kilograms, with automatic calculation of landfill diversion rate and trend analysis over time.
Evaluation matrix scoring each supplier on packaging, certifications, transport miles, ethical practices, and waste reduction commitments. Used during supplier review and procurement decisions.
One-page report template showing key metrics — cups saved, waste diverted, energy consumption, carbon estimate — with quarter-on-quarter comparison and targets for the next period.
Comprehensive policy document template covering energy management, waste hierarchy, water conservation, ethical sourcing standards, packaging commitments, and community engagement goals.
Step-by-step launch plan covering incentive model selection, cup sourcing, till setup, signage design, staff training schedule, and promotional timeline for the first month.
Standard operating procedure for accepting, inspecting, and serving into customer-brought reusable cups, including refusal criteria, handling protocols, and borrow cup sanitisation requirements.
Log tracking borrow cup deposits issued and returns received, identifying unreturned cups, calculating loss rate, and flagging when stock needs replenishing.
Monthly report pulling till data to show reusable cup transactions as a percentage of total takeaway drinks, with trend lines, day-of-week analysis, and progress against quarterly targets.
Procurement brief template for sourcing branded reusable cups, covering design specifications, supplier comparison, minimum order quantities, pricing, margin targets, and lead times.

Assessment matrix scoring each team member across espresso technique, milk texturing, grinder calibration, sensory vocabulary, customer communication, and manual brew methods. Used to assign training tiers and track progression.

12-week structured curriculum template with session plans for Foundation, Intermediate, and Advanced tiers. Each session includes learning objectives, theory content, practical exercises, and assessment criteria.
Per-person log tracking training sessions attended, skills demonstrated, assessment scores, certifications earned, and development goals. Reviewed monthly during one-to-one meetings.
Step-by-step guide for running a cupping session following SCA protocol: sample preparation, water temperature, grind size, ratio, breaking the crust, and scoring using standardised cupping forms.

Calibration assessment combining written questions on coffee theory with practical blind tasting and brewing challenges. Designed to identify knowledge gaps and inform the next quarter's training focus.

Step-by-step guide for running a cupping session: sample preparation (dose, grind, water temperature), timing, crust-breaking technique, evaluation sequence, and team discussion framework following SCA protocol.
Per-coffee reference card recording the target espresso recipe (dose, yield, time, temperature), flavour profile descriptors, and acceptable variance ranges. Used as the morning dial-in reference.
Ongoing log tracking each coffee evaluated: roast date, cupping date, SCA scores by category, espresso parameters, flavour notes, and quality trajectory across the bag's lifespan.
Quarterly report template for blind-cupping competitor coffees alongside own offerings, recording scores, tasting notes, price comparisons, and strategic insights on market positioning.
Structured triangulation and threshold detection exercises for team sensory calibration, including scoring comparison forms to track inter-rater agreement and individual progression over time.

Complete production guide covering coffee selection, grind size, ratio, steeping time, filtration method, storage, labelling, shelf-life management, and quality checks for consistent cold brew batch production.
Standardised recipe cards for every cold drink on the menu: ingredients, quantities, ice amounts, preparation steps, serve ware specification, and garnish. Laminated for bar station use.
Batch tracking log recording brew date, coffee used, grind setting, water volume, steep time, yield volume, taste assessment, expiry date, and waste. Used to optimise production scheduling.
Quarterly planning template for seasonal cold drink development: concept, recipe testing schedule, ingredient sourcing, cost analysis, pricing, promotional plan, and launch date coordination.
Spreadsheet calculating cost-per-serve for each cold drink including coffee, milk, ice, syrups, cups, and lids, with automatic margin percentage and recommended pricing based on target profitability.
Laminated recipe card for each coffee showing dose, grind setting, water volume, brew time, maximum hold time, and flavour profile notes. Positioned at the brew station for reference during service.
Daily log tracking each batch brewed: time brewed, coffee used, grind setting, discard time, cups served, and waste volume. Used to optimise production scheduling and minimise waste.
Planning template for the rotating filter coffee programme: scheduled coffees by week, origin and process details, order quantities, roast dates, and changeover dates to ensure seamless transitions.
A5 card template displayed next to the batch brewer showing the current coffee's name, origin, farm or cooperative, process, roast profile, and three flavour descriptors in accessible language.
Spreadsheet calculating batch brew cost-per-serve including coffee cost, water, filters, and waste factor, with margin analysis and recommended pricing to achieve profitability targets.
Year-view calendar showing four seasonal windows with planned specials, launch dates, retirement dates, ingredient ordering deadlines, and promotional milestones for the entire year.
Standardised template for developing new specials: concept name, inspiration, ingredients, method, cost-per-serve, allergens, presentation spec, and sign-off by manager before launch.

Pre-launch checklist covering: recipe card completed, all staff trained, ingredients ordered and received, till button created, signage installed, social media content scheduled, and opening day quality check.
Daily sales tracker for each seasonal special showing units sold, revenue, cost-of-goods, margin, and customer feedback notes. Used for post-season performance review and future planning.
Historical archive of every seasonal special launched, including recipe, cost data, sales performance, customer feedback summary, and lessons learned. Referenced when planning future seasons.
Structured evaluation matrix scoring bakery candidates across product quality, range, pricing, delivery reliability, food safety credentials, allergen documentation, insurance, and brand alignment.

Daily checklist for inspecting bakery deliveries: order accuracy, quantity verification, visual quality check, temperature check for chilled items, packaging integrity, and sign-off with issue flagging.
Standardised report for documenting delivery problems: issue type (late, missing items, quality failure, damaged goods), photo evidence, immediate action taken, and credit or replacement requested.
Planning template matching previous week's sales data to upcoming order quantities, with adjustments for weather, events, and seasonal factors. Reduces over-ordering and minimises waste.
Agenda and minutes template for quarterly bakery partner reviews: quality performance, delivery metrics, pricing review, new product pipeline, seasonal planning, and any issues requiring resolution.

Visual process diagram showing every step in the drive-through journey: approach, order point, payment, preparation, assembly, handoff, and departure, with role assignments and timing targets at each stage.

Role-by-role guide for each drive-through station: order taker, barista, food assembler, payment handler, and window runner. Covers responsibilities, communication protocols, and peak-period escalation.
Hourly tracker capturing transactions per hour, average service time, order errors, queue length at peak intervals, and upsell success rate. Feeds into weekly performance review.
Design specification for the drive-through menu board: item hierarchy, pricing layout, font sizes for vehicle-distance readability, meal deal groupings, and seasonal special placement.

Assessment form for mystery shopper visits scoring: queue wait time, greeting quality, order accuracy, service time, product quality and temperature, presentation, upsell attempt, and farewell interaction.
Floor plan template for mapping equipment, storage, and movement paths within a compact kiosk footprint. Includes reach-zone analysis and step-count optimisation for the primary operator position.
Site-specific checklist covering equipment startup, stock check, milk prep, till float, and cleanliness for opening, plus shutdown, stock count, waste recording, cleaning, and security for closing.
Daily stock management template: closing stock count, next-day sales forecast by item, order calculation, delivery confirmation, and variance tracking to minimise both stockouts and waste.
Comprehensive SOP for kiosk operation covering solo workflow, peak-hour batching, customer queue management, food safety protocols, handwashing procedures, and emergency contingencies.
Weekly dashboard tracking kiosk-specific metrics: revenue per square metre, drinks per staff hour, peak throughput rate, waste rate, and customer count with trend lines for continuous improvement.

Step-by-step laminated checklists for van deployment and pack-down: vehicle positioning, utility connections, equipment startup sequence, stock staging, and reverse for teardown including waste disposal.

Per-location log recording each trading day: revenue, pitch fee, travel cost, fuel, drinks served, weather conditions, and calculated net profit. Used to rank locations and optimise the weekly schedule.

Complete booking workflow: enquiry response template, quote calculator (per-head or flat fee), booking confirmation with deposit terms, event logistics checklist (power, water, access), and post-event invoice.
