Annual maintenance calendar tracking MOT, service intervals, generator maintenance, water tank sanitisation, gas safety inspection, tyre checks, and pre-journey vehicle condition checks.

Register of all licences, permits, and certificates with issue dates, expiry dates, renewal reminders, and responsible authority contact details. Covers street trading consents, food registration, insurance, gas, and electrical safety.

Planning template for monthly throwdowns: competition category, rules, judging criteria, equipment needed, prize, and results recording. Includes latte art, espresso accuracy, blind tasting, and speed challenge formats.
8-week structured preparation plan for SCA-sanctioned competitions: weekly milestones for signature drink development, technical routine practice, presentation rehearsal, and equipment preparation.

Policy document defining how the cafe supports competitions: selection criteria for external entry, financial support terms, practice time allocation, equipment provision, and post-competition knowledge sharing requirements.
Budget template covering all competition costs: entry fees, practice coffee, equipment, travel, accommodation, and incidentals, with annual budget allocation and per-competition cost tracking.
Historical log of all internal and external competitions: date, category, participants, results, key learnings, skills brought back to the cafe, and marketing content generated from each event.

Comprehensive audit listing every task, decision, relationship, and knowledge area that currently depends on the owner. Scored by criticality and ease of delegation to identify priority areas for succession work.

Crisis document providing step-by-step instructions for running the cafe during unplanned owner absence: financial access, supplier contacts, urgent decision authority, staff management, and escalation contacts.
Formal succession strategy document covering preferred transition route, successor identification, development milestones, financial preparation, timeline, and annual review schedule.

Milestone tracker for the successor's development journey: skills acquired, responsibilities delegated, independent operation periods completed, and readiness assessment at 6, 12, and 18-month intervals.

Preparation checklist for owners pursuing external sale: financial records clean-up, lease review, asset inventory, business structure optimisation, valuation preparation, and broker engagement steps.

Database of all identified pop-up and market opportunities: event name, frequency, pitch fee, expected footfall, logistics (power, water, cover), past performance, and priority ranking for the annual calendar.
End-to-end checklist covering opportunity assessment, pre-event preparation (equipment, stock, staff, transport), event-day setup and operations workflow, teardown, and post-event review triggers.
Complete inventory of portable equipment, supplies, and branding materials stored in the pop-up kit, with condition checks, replacement triggers, and pre-event verification checklist.

Per-event financial tracker: pitch fee, staff cost, product cost, transport, revenue, profit or loss, drinks served, and revenue-per-hour. Aggregated annually to assess overall pop-up programme profitability.
Structured review completed within 48 hours of each event: financial performance, operational lessons, customer feedback, equipment issues, and decision on whether to repeat the event.

Spreadsheet calculating per-delivery costs (product, packaging, delivery time, fuel) and pricing for each package type, ensuring minimum 40% margin with sensitivity analysis for different order sizes.

Standard operating procedure for the complete delivery workflow: order receipt, preparation scheduling, quality checks, packaging, route planning, delivery handoff protocol, and post-delivery feedback collection.

CRM-style tracker for corporate accounts: company name, contact person, standing order details, delivery schedule, payment terms, last review date, revenue to date, and notes on preferences or special requirements.

Sales materials for approaching corporate prospects: one-page service overview, menu with pricing, ordering instructions, and testimonials from existing clients. Available as print and digital versions.

Structured review template for quarterly client check-ins: satisfaction assessment, order pattern analysis, upcoming needs (events, seasonal requirements), upsell opportunities, and relationship health score.

Monthly dashboard comparing the cafe's key financial metrics (COGS %, staff cost %, rent %, net margin, ATV, revenue per seat) against published UK cafe industry averages, with traffic-light indicators for each metric.

Structured assessment form for evaluating competitor and aspirational cafes: exterior, interior, menu, coffee quality, food, service, cleanliness, digital presence, and innovation, with numerical scoring and comparison notes.

Ongoing database of competitor information: pricing by product category, menu changes, marketing campaigns, renovations, online review trends, and notable innovations. Updated quarterly with dated entries.

Report template synthesising financial performance vs. benchmarks, competitor landscape changes, aspirational visit insights, industry trend observations, and recommended strategic actions for the next quarter.

Assessment checklist used by mystery shoppers to evaluate the cafe alongside its top 3 competitors on identical criteria: approach, greeting, order process, wait time, product quality, ambience, cleanliness, and overall impression.
